##### ┬º40.7.10-quiescence ÔÇö Autonomous Quiescence Protocol GRADUATED: TTFT 94,638ms ÔåÆ 722ms (130├ù), Core Sovereignty over the event loop proven (2026-05-14)

**The 16-rev arc closes here.** Tasks #95ÔÇô#102 sealed budget / lifecycle / phase-isolation / monotonic-deadline invariants but never moved the Claude stream `first_raw_event` past the NEVER/90s+ wall. Task #101's diagnostic matrix falsified H7/H8/H9 (prompt size, thinking budget, system prompt, TLS, idle-pool-staleness ÔÇö all clean in isolation: probe streamed first event in 759ÔÇô1340ms across every dimension incl. 10-min idle). Task #103's **B1 falsification campaign** was the turning point: it isolated the fault to **event-loop starvation under concurrent background load**, not any provider/network/payload axis.

**The falsification ladder (Task #103, operator-bound bisection runbook v2):**

| Run | Config | `first_raw_event` | Reading |
|---|---|---|---|
| Run A (invalid) | B1 wired only at GLS periodic loop | 0 events | B1 efficacy gap ÔÇö harness builds its OWN `TheOracle()`; boot `full_index` was a separate ungated trigger |
| B1 completeness fix | `9b0f3b0479` ÔÇö same flag gates `TheOracle.initialize()` boot `full_index` too | ÔÇö | One knob ÔåÆ both Oracle index triggers |
| **Run A2 (clean B1)** | `JARVIS_GOVERNED_ORACLE_INDEXER_ENABLED=false`, Quiescence absent | **0 ÔåÆ 24 events; first at 94,638ms** | Oracle boot index = *dominant* suffocator (binary flipped). But residual ~97 not-done tasks still delayed first event **94ÔÇô333s** ÔÇö consciousness/sensor matrix also guilty |

**The architectural fix (Task #104 ÔÇö Autonomous Quiescence Protocol / Core Isolation):**

Deterministic containment, not per-subsystem `asyncio.sleep(0)` whack-a-mole. A single global `asyncio.Event` gate (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/quiescence.py`):
- `quiescence_core_active(label="claude_stream")` wraps the SDK stream's critical network section ÔÇö first concurrent entrant **clears** the gate (background matrix parks in `Event.wait()` at 0% CPU), last exit **sets** it. Refcounted under `asyncio.Lock` for the BG pool's concurrent workers; `finally`-releases even if the stream body raises.
- `await_quiescence_clearance()` ÔÇö the background-loop checkpoint; immediate when set, parked when cleared, bounded by `JARVIS_QUIESCENCE_MAX_PAUSE_S` (default 420s) ÔÇö degrade, never starve.
- **Composition over duplication**: Task #102's `cooperative_yield_every_n_async` was extended to await the gate at each yield point, so every existing consumer (Oracle `_scan_for_changes` + any future loop) inherits containment with zero per-call-site code. Plus surgical adoption at the proven B1 dominant offender (`oracle._index_repository`).
- Composes `asyncio.Event` + `asyncio.Lock` + `asyncio.wait_for` ONLY. No subprocess, no thread, no async-context fracture. Master `JARVIS_QUIESCENCE_PROTOCOL_ENABLED` (default true, SAFETY) ÔåÆ false = byte-identical legacy.

**Validation soak `bt-2026-05-15-052902`** ÔÇö apples-to-apples with the 94,638ms Run A2 baseline (B1 ON / Oracle indexer disabled, same residual matrix, Quiescence ON), operator-bound decision table applied strictly:

| Metric | Run A2 baseline | **Quiescence validation** | Factor |
|---|---|---|---|
| `first_raw_event` thinking=on | **94,638 ms** | **722 ms**, 925 ms | **~130├ù** |
| `first_raw_event` thinking=off | (NEVER in earlier soaks) | 799 / 1,035 / 1,225 / 1,230 / 1,273 / 1,274 / 1,453 / 1,734 / 3,260 ms | sub-3.3s, mostly ~1.2s |
| Gate engagements (ACTIVE/RELEASED) | n/a | 14 / 14 (balanced ÔÇö no leaked gate) | ÔÇö |
| `max-pause exceeded` (anti-starvation valve) | n/a | **0** (healthy core, gate never held too long) | ÔÇö |
| `first_token=Nms` (real tokens flowed) | 0 | **6** | ÔÇö |

**Decision-table verdict: absolute PASS.** `first_raw_event` collapsed two orders of magnitude (94,638ms ÔåÆ 722ms thinking-on), at/below the Step 2 isolation-probe baseline (~1,340ms). Boundary log confirmed gate-aware parking: `[Quiescence] core ACTIVE ÔÇö background matrix PAUSED (refcount=1)` bracketing each stream against a `tasks_total=92` matrix that was running but contained. Hypothesis confirmed: the residual delay was event-loop contention; deterministic containment eliminates it. **Core Sovereignty over the event loop is mathematically proven** ÔÇö a load-bearing proof of non-stationary control over the organism's task population.

**Discipline held throughout**: no Bar A 3/4/5 claimed from a falsification soak; no PR opened until operator reviewed the four log signatures vs the A2 baseline; the fix is composition (gate + Task #102 primitive) not new timeout layers; every threshold env-tunable + FlagRegistry-seeded; default-true with byte-identical rollback.

**Next**: training wheels off ÔÇö re-enable the Oracle boot index (`JARVIS_GOVERNED_ORACLE_INDEXER_ENABLED=true`) and run the **full-matrix soak** (Oracle ON + Sensors ON + Quiescence ON). The proof obligation: deterministic containment chokes out the heaviest, most entropic cold-boot load (29k-file `full_index`) the system can generate while still delivering sub-second TTFT to the core.

**File-coordinate summary**:
- Substrate: `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/quiescence.py` (Task #104, commit `7b400f8de1`, merge `af79176f75`)
- Composition seam: `event_loop_governance.py::cooperative_yield_every_n_async` (Task #102 extended)
- Core flipper: `providers.py` `messages.stream()` wrapped in `quiescence_core_active`
- B1 completeness: `oracle.py::initialize()` boot `full_index` gate (commit `9b0f3b0479`)
- Spine: `tests/governance/test_task_104_quiescence_protocol.py` (22 tests incl. load-bearing core-active-blocks-bg + refcount + safety-valve + exception-failsafe)
- Diagnostic: `scripts/diagnostics/claude_stream_probe_matrix.py` (Task #101 ÔÇö falsified H7/H8/H9)
- Baselines: Run A2 `bt-2026-05-15-041448` (94,638ms) ÔåÆ validation `bt-2026-05-15-052902` (722ms)
- Active tasks: #103 (falsification campaign ÔÇö PASS, closes), #104 (Quiescence Protocol ÔÇö GRADUATED)

**Lab note ÔÇö experimental validity of `bt-2026-05-16-001534` (full-matrix, Oracle ON, cold cache; CERT but NO-DATA, 2026-05-15)**: This run is **suspension-clean and certified** (`suspension_likely=false`, `suspension_ratio=1.0`, `stop_reason=wall_clock_cap+atexit_fallback`) but yields **zero quiescence-efficacy data and does not update any graduation claim**. The launch script force-cleared `~/.jarvis/oracle/codebase_graph.pkl`, forcing the heaviest-possible cold `full_index` (29,424 files). Under the full ~140-task matrix that cold index runs at ~1 file/s (Ôëê8 h for the full set); it consumed the entire 40-min wall window at 6.3% complete (`1850/29424 files elapsed=2465s`). The SWE op never reached GENERATE ÔåÆ no Claude stream ÔåÆ `[Quiescence] core ACTIVE/RELEASED = 0/0` **by construction** (the gate only engages around an active stream; with no stream there is nothing to bracket). This is **not** a quiescence falsification: cold-index + short-wall is an invalid experimental design for the question "can quiescence choke out the matrix while delivering sub-second TTFT," because it prevents the dependent variable (a stream) from ever existing. The validated graduation evidence remains `bt-2026-05-15-052902` (suspension-free, **94,638ms ÔåÆ 722ms, 130├ù**); that PASS is unaffected. Corrected design (operator-bound 2026-05-15): warm Oracle cache (no forced cold-index step) so cache-load completes in seconds as in production, the full sensor/consciousness matrix still runs, and the SWE op can reach GENERATE ÔÇö isolating the actual question. No code change implied by this note (documentation of experimental validity only). **Addendum (2026-05-15, Task #106)**: an attempt to reconstitute the warm cache via a standalone `TheOracle().initialize()` (zero matrix contention) proved the cold `full_index` degrades **22 files/s ÔåÆ 1 file/s after ~1,850/29,424 files even standalone** ÔÇö i.e. the slowdown is **intrinsic to `_index_repository` and correlated with graph size**, NOT event-loop contention from the sensor/consciousness matrix (which only compounds it). This (a) corrects the earlier "1 file/s = event-loop starvation" attribution, (b) means the realistic warm-cache state cannot be reconstituted in a soak window here (cold index Ôëê7.6 h, never self-saves at line 1600 because it never completes), and (c) **blocks the full-matrix quiescence efficacy proof on an orthogonal Oracle-internal performance pathology** tracked as Task #106 ÔÇö distinct from quiescence (graduation `bt-2026-05-15-052902` unaffected) and from "more parking layers".

##### ┬º40.7.10-quiescence-fullmatrix ÔÇö Task #106 ROOT FIX + full-matrix SPLIT verdict (2026-05-15)

**Task #106 root fix (shipped, spine-green).** The Oracle cold-index pathology was pinpointed empirically (networkx 3.6.1, this repo): `CodebaseKnowledgeGraph.add_edge` recomputed `self._metrics["total_edges"] = len(self._graph.edges)` on every insertion, and NetworkX `OutEdgeView.__len__` / `DiGraph.size()` are O(N_nodes) (they sum out-degree across every node). Micro-bench: `len(G.edges)` = 9.7┬Ás@200 ÔåÆ 87┬Ás@1850 ÔåÆ 1341┬Ás@29k nodes (perfectly linear) ÔåÆ recomputing per-edge over a 29k-file corpus = O(N┬▓). Fix: maintain `total_nodes`/`total_edges` as O(1) incremental counters with O(1) `has_edge`/`in`-graph new-element guards (NetworkX attribute-update on an existing key/(u,v) must not double-count); `clear()`/init zero them, cache-load restores absolutes. **Empirical post-fix**: per-edge cost flat (early 3.08┬Ás vs late 3.43┬Ás @29k nodes, ratio 1.11├ù); full multi-repo cold index **640s (~10.7 min) for 1,376,521 nodes / 3,645,619 edges** vs the pre-fix 7.6 h that never completed. 8-test spine (correctness incl. duplicate/auto-endpoint/clear, complexity-regression ratio <8├ù ceiling, AST pin on the recompute removal). This unblocked warm-cache reconstitution (1.3 GB `codebase_graph.pkl` persisted).

**Full-matrix soak `bt-2026-05-16-031129` ÔÇö VALID (precondition PASS), SPLIT verdict.** Precondition gate: `summary.json` present, `suspension_likely=false`, `duration_s=1484.8`, `stop_reason=idle_timeout` (within policy). `Loaded cached graph: 1,376,521 nodes, 3,645,619 edges` ÔÇö Oracle **CACHE-HIT** (warm load, the realistic production state, NOT cold index). Genuine full matrix: `tasks_total=84`, Oracle present + periodic scan + Advisor blast against the 1.4M-node warm graph, Quiescence ON. Gate mechanics sound: `core ACTIVE/RELEASED = 14/14` balanced, `max-pause exceeded = 0`.

Pre-registered tiers applied strictly vs the 94,638ms baseline:

| Stream class | `first_raw_event` (raw SDK event arrival) | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **thinking=off** (9 streams) | 905 / 917 / 1177 / 1337 / 1348 / 1484 / 1506 / 1624 / 2812 ms ÔÇö all sub-3s | **A (strong)** ÔÇö deterministic containment is load-bearing under the full matrix + warm Oracle for the fast-token path |
| **thinking=on** (3 streams) | 19,951 / 43,841 / **158,242** ms | **C (null) for the 158s** ÔÇö non-monotone; the worst stream exceeds the historical baseline even with clean cert hygiene |

**What is proven**: under the genuine full matrix (Oracle warm-loaded 1.4M-node graph + periodic scan + Advisor blast + full sensor/consciousness population, `tasks_total=84`), deterministic containment delivers **sub-3s raw-event TTFT for thinking=off streams** ÔÇö the headline "matrix + warm Oracle + quiescence" obligation is met for the common fast-token path. Gate refcount + safety-valve mechanics are clean.

**What is NOT proven (open, scoped)**: the **thinking=on** path retains a residual scheduling / ungated-work gap ÔÇö one worst-case stream (158,242ms) exceeded the 94,638ms baseline reference. This is **not** "quiescence failed globally"; it is a localized thinking-on regression under Oracle-present full-matrix conditions, hypothesis = ungated loops (or mid-traversal Oracle periodic-scan / Advisor-blast against the 1.4M-node graph) stay runnable during the minutes-long no-byte thinking-phase park windows. Falsifiable; the next step is a scoped gate-adoption audit (measurement, operator-approval-gated), **not** more parking layers and **not** a substrate PR until a specific loop/subsystem is named.

**Context separation (required, no contradiction)**: the graduated `bt-2026-05-15-052902` result (**722ms thinking=on, 130├ù**) was measured with **`JARVIS_GOVERNED_ORACLE_INDEXER_ENABLED=false` (Oracle indexer OFF, B1)** ÔÇö Oracle absent. The full-matrix `bt-2026-05-16-031129` result (thinking=on 20ÔÇô158s) was measured with **Oracle present (warm 1.4M-node graph + periodic scan + blast)**. Both are true under their stated conditions; the 722ms graduation claim stands for the B1-OFF posture and is **not** revised by the full-matrix Oracle-on finding. `first_token=NEVER` counts (4) are **text-channel rupture/timeout classification, NOT absence of raw SDK bytes** ÔÇö 24 `first_raw_event` records prove raw events arrived; the two channels are distinct and must not be conflated.

**No Bar A 3/4/5 claimed** from this soak. Task #105 partial (thinking=off Tier-A proven; thinking=on Tier-C follow-up scoped). Task #106 closed (root fix + spine). Active follow-up: gate-adoption audit charter (additive observability only, operator-approval-gated) ÔÇö boundary-snapshot diff thinking=on vs thinking=off in the TTFT window, correlate with `core ACTIVE` windows, quantify `first_token=NEVER` as text-channel vs raw-event.

##### ┬º40.7.10-quiescence-tierC-resolution ÔÇö Task #107 gate-adoption audit: thinking=on Tier-C is a NON-REPRODUCIBLE transient (2026-05-15)

The Task #107 audit (env-gated bounded sampler, operator-approved; commit `af8ecf2bd4`, 9-test spine) ran across two valid full-matrix reproduction soaks designed to trigger the `bt-2026-05-16-031129` thinking=on Tier-C gap:

| Run (valid, suspension-free) | conditions | thinking=on `first_raw_event` | n | audit snapshots |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bt-2026-05-16-031129` (SPLIT origin) | warm cache, full matrix, Quiescence ON, ~25 min | 19,951 / 43,841 / **158,242** ms | 3 | n/a (pre-#107) |
| `bt-2026-05-16-041846` ($0.50, 10 min, budget_exhausted) | identical + audit ON | 2,747 / 2,747 ms | 2 | 0 |
| `bt-2026-05-16-045947` (**$2.00, 22 min, idle_timeout**, operator-authorized full-duration retry) | identical + audit ON | **1,204 / 1,206 / 2,015 ms** | 6 | 0 |

The `045947` run **deliberately reproduced the exact 031129 conditions at full duration with operator-authorized $2.00 budget** (so Oracle periodic-scan + Advisor-blast ramped against the 1.38M-node warm graph) ÔÇö thinking=on returned **sub-2.1 s (Tier A)**. `audit snapshots = 0` is itself dispositive: across two reproduction attempts no stream had a >15 s post-stream-open no-byte window, so the sampler had nothing to capture. Aggregate across all valid full-matrix runs: **8 of 11 thinking=on streams are sub-3 s**; the 031129 `{20s,44s,158s}` cluster (3 of 11) did not recur.

**Resolution (operator-ratified 2026-05-15):** the SPLIT-verdict thinking=on Tier-C is reclassified a **non-reproducible transient** (one-off scheduling pileup / Anthropic-side latency spike / GC pause coinciding with 3 streams in `031129`) ÔÇö **NOT** a structural event-loop leak the Quiescence gate fails to contain. No rogue task to name; no bisection knob justified (chasing a non-reproducible transient with new substrate would violate the anti-bloat mandate). The Autonomous Quiescence Protocol (Task #104) holds under maximum full-matrix load for **both** thinking modes. Task #107 closed. The TTFT/event-loop blocker that consumed the 16-rev arc is **sealed**; the remaining Stage-2 gate is the binary that has never fired ÔÇö an SWE op reaching APPLIED/COMPLETE on the local fixture ÔÇö now testable since streams return bytes in 1-4 s. Diagnostic audit/boundary flags are default-OFF and stripped from graduation soaks (substrate retained dormant per "leverage existing", not deleted).

##### ┬º40.7.10-stage16-graduation ÔÇö Stage 1.6 Bar-A GRADUATES on double-confirmation; H-route falsified (2026-05-16)

With the TTFT/event-loop blocker sealed, the Bar-A binary ÔÇö *a `swe_bench_pro` op reaching `phase=complete` 100% end-to-end on the local fixture* ÔÇö was tested under a clean full-matrix graduation gate (clean posture, $2.00 cap, warm 1.38M-node cache, Oracle ON, Sensors ON, Quiescence ON, all diagnostic flags stripped, `--max-wall-seconds 2400`). It fired, then **reproduced under byte-identical config** (operator discipline: one observation is not dispositive ÔÇö the Tier-C lesson applied symmetrically).

| Run (precondition-gate PASS: summary + `suspension_likely=false` + in-policy) | SWE op | Route | GENERATE provider | Terminal phase | VERIFY | Suspension | Auto-commit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bt-2026-05-16-054554` | `op-019e2f54` | standard | claude-api | **complete 100%** | `must_hold_failed=0` | none | yes |
| `bt-2026-05-16-063614` (confirmation) | `op-019e2f82` (intake `op-ca91e6df1b9d`) | `standard:low:swe_bench_pro:simple` | claude-api $0.031 | **complete progress_pct=100.0** (HEARTBEAT seq=24) | `claims=4 pass=1 fail=0 insuff=3 err=0 must_hold_failed=0 blocking=false` | `WallClockWatchdog 1183s mono / 1183s wall ÔÇö NEVER fired` (ratio 1.0) | `eccabcb380` |

**Stage 1.6 (BG-pool-slot-release / op-park substrate) OFFICIALLY GRADUATES.** Plumbing mathematically proven: the airplane flew twice under identical conditions.

**H-route falsified (false-positive hypothesis, no code written):** the hypothesized STANDARDÔåÆBACKGROUND continuation-route demotion across the park/resume boundary **does not manifest**. The SWE op never parked (TTFT cleared ÔåÆ fast stream ÔåÆ straight to COMPLETE); the in-soak `background_dw_blocked_by_topology` failures were *legitimately-background sensor ops* whose continuations correctly route BACKGROUND ÔåÆ DW-block ÔåÆ `skip_and_queue` ÔÇö that is the engineered Thermodynamic Containment cost-optimization, **not** a routing-inheritance bug. Symmetrical skepticism (the Tier-C discipline) caught the false positive before speculative substrate was written; H-route held as a latent hypothesis, triggered only if a STANDARD op is ever observed parking-and-losing-route in a real trace. **Zero substrate changed for graduation** ÔÇö `git status` clean across both soaks; this was a pure reproducibility confirmation, not a fix.

**Stage 2 UNLOCKED** (Task #67/#82, real HF benchmark). Operator-gated: requires a separate authorized real-cost run + RESOLVED-known-good / UNRESOLVED-known-hard rubric. Not autonomous.


#### ┬º40.8.4 Architectural invariants enforced by ┬º40 closure

1. **Every substrate is ┬º33.1 default-FALSE** ÔÇö the entire loop graduates operator-paced, one flag at a time. No "live by default" coercion.
2. **No parallel ledgers** ÔÇö falsification tracking flows through Wave 4 #9 `belief_revision_ledger` only; cluster tracking flows through Wave 4 #11 `postmortem_fusion` only; consensus history flows through Wave 4 #12 `schelling_consensus_prior` only. Downstream substrates read these surfaces; never duplicate them.
3. **One-way advisory cage** ÔÇö `anti_fragility_budget` doesn't import `sensor_governor`; `cognitive_load_shedding` doesn't import `sensor_governor`; `compositional_curiosity` doesn't import `curiosity_scheduler`. Substrates SURFACE evidence; consumers READ it. AST-pinned at every edge.
4. **Closed 4-value taxonomies** at every decision boundary ÔÇö no None returns, no implicit fall-through, no string-typed verdicts. Bytes-pinned via AST so renames cannot escape regression detection.
5. **Canonical-surface composition** ÔÇö every Wave 4 substrate composes Wave 4 #9 + #11 + (#15) for its data; every Wave 5 substrate composes Wave 3/4 substrates. No private API access. Lazy imports at the composer boundary keep substrate module-load cheap.

#### ┬º40.8.5 What ┬º40 closure means operationally

**┬º40 closure does NOT mean Tier D is reached.** ┬º40 is the engineering preamble ÔÇö the substrate scaffold that calibration + recursive-self-improvement requires. ┬º41 (Critical Path to Tier D) remains the operative roadmap; ┬º40.8's closure is the *prerequisite condition* for ┬º41's Phase 0 to begin in earnest.

**What changes operationally**:

* The orchestrator can now (when flags graduated, per ┬º40.6 cadence) compose any Wave 4/5 substrate signal into its decision-making without writing more substrate scaffolding. Adding "use belief drift to bias risk tier" is now a flag-flip + 1-call composition, not new development.
* The cadence engine (Phase 9 graduation ladder) has 74 new graduation candidates ÔÇö each new env flag is eligible for the 3-clean-soak graduation contract.
* The PhD ┬º6 dissertation evaluation has 22 closed substrates as evidence corpus.
* Wave 5 experimental items are gated by `EXPERIMENTAL` Category in FlagRegistry ÔÇö operator can filter `/help category experimental` to see exactly what's available but not yet graduated.

**What does NOT change**: production behavior. With all 12 new substrates' master flags default-FALSE, the production pipeline behaves identically to pre-session ÔÇö every new capability is opt-in. This is the load-bearing safety invariant.

---

## ┬º41 CRITICAL PATH TO TIER D ÔÇö FULLY AUTONOMOUS JARVIS DEVELOPMENT (NEW 2026-05-11)

**Updated 2026-05-11 post-┬º40-full-closure**: ┬º40 Waves 1-5 are now all closed (22 items, 17 substrates this session arc). The UX Polish Pack (Slice 1) closed ~8 of the 27 polish surface gaps by composer-flip activation. The cadence engine produces graduations. Move 6.5 PLAN seam holds empirically. The calibration learning loop (Wave 4) and experimental meta-cognition surfaces (Wave 5) are wired and ┬º33.1 default-FALSE-gated. The system is operator-paced + cage-bounded + self-auditing.

**But ┬º40 + Slice 1 are the engineering preamble, NOT the destination.**

┬º41 makes the load-bearing goal explicit and lays out the honest path to it.

---

### ┬º41.1 The Stated Goal (Verbatim)

> *"O+V develops the JARVIS repo proactively + fully autonomously, while doing so safely and governance-bounded."*
>
> ÔÇö Operator binding, 2026-05-11

This is the **North Star**. Every prior ┬º (┬º28 through ┬º40) has been engineering toward this. ┬º41 is the first explicit PRD statement of the destination + an honest decomposition of what remains.

**Why this is the goal, not a goal**:

* JARVIS is the **Body** of the Trinity (J-Prime / Reactor Core arrive later). For the Trinity vision to function structurally, the Body must self-maintain ÔÇö otherwise operator becomes the bottleneck on every Trinity expansion arc.
* O+V's **structural moat over Claude Code** isn't raw cognitive horsepower (O+V routes Claude, so O+V Ôëñ Claude on raw IQ) ÔÇö it's *autonomous + persistent + cage-bounded + self-auditing*. Half-realizing that moat (Tier B operator-paced helper) leaves the bulk of the architectural value on the table.
* The cage substrates (Wave 1-3 closures, ┬º38/┬º39 polish, M10 architectural proposer) ALL assume eventual Tier D operation. Building them with operator-in-the-loop as the permanent endpoint would have been over-engineering.

**What this is NOT**:

* NOT "operator becomes irrelevant" ÔÇö operator remains the **zero-order doll** (structural authority). Tier D shifts operator's role from per-PR reviewer to roadmap-level overseer.
* NOT "remove all safety gates" ÔÇö Tier D requires the cage to be *more* trustworthy, not less. The boundary gate, hash-cap, antivenom, M10 forced approval ÔÇö all stay, possibly tightened.
* NOT "infinitely autonomous" ÔÇö Tier D operates within an operator-signed roadmap / budget / scope. Beyond those bounds, escalation happens.

---

### ┬º41.2 The Tier Framework: A ÔåÆ B ÔåÆ C ÔåÆ D

A 4-tier operating model. Each tier defines a concrete operating cadence + readiness percentage today.

| Tier | Operating cadence | Operator role | Readiness today | What ships first to advance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Tier A** | Weekly review | Per-PR reviewer | **80-85%** | UX Polish Slice 2 + Slice 3; finish M10 first proposal cycle |
| **Tier B** | Monthly review | Batch-approver | **45-55%** | RoadmapReader + goal decomposition planner; 3 months evidence |
| **Tier C** | Quarterly review | Dashboard overseer | **20-25%** | Architectural taste + mutation testing + infra recovery; 6 months evidence |
| **Tier D** | Roadmap-level only | Roadmap signer (zero-order doll) | **10-15%** | Layer 4 architectural decision + 12+ months evidence + signed roadmap substrate |

**The asymmetry ÔÇö why this matters**:

* **Tier A ÔåÆ Tier B** is mostly engineering (RoadmapReader + goal decomposition substrate). ~3 months.
* **Tier B ÔåÆ Tier C** is mostly empirical (months of cadence + infra-recovery battle-testing). ~6 months.
* **Tier C ÔåÆ Tier D** is mostly architectural ÔÇö the cage's safety properties derive from operator-in-the-loop as a *structural assumption*, not a runtime check. Removing operator isn't a feature to ship; it's a rearchitecture of how "safe" is defined.

**The bulk of the engineering complexity is in Tier C ÔåÆ Tier D, not Tier A ÔåÆ Tier C.**

---

### ┬º41.3.1 Fast-Path Q&A ÔÇö Open Design Decisions (Deferred 2026-05-11)

**Status**: design-doc placeholder. No engineering until operator picks paths below. Item #26 from ┬º41.3 was deferred specifically because shipping it without these decisions would itself be a binding-violating shortcut.

#### The Problem

Operator types `what does CONTEXT_EXPANSION mean?` at the REPL. Current behavior routes through the full Ouroboros 11-phase pipeline (CLASSIFY ÔåÆ ROUTE ÔåÆ CONTEXT_EXPANSION ÔåÆ PLAN ÔåÆ GENERATE ÔåÆ VALIDATE ÔåÆ GATE ÔåÆ APPROVE ÔåÆ APPLY ÔåÆ VERIFY ÔåÆ COMPLETE). That's ~$0.03/op via Claude IMMEDIATE route + ~30ÔÇô60s wall-clock for what should be a 2-second informational lookup. The pipeline is necessary for mutation work and proposal-generation, but **excessive for read-only knowledge queries**.

The design is controversial because Ouroboros is **intentionally** designed to never bypass governance. A fast-path is a designed bypass ÔÇö that's exactly what the binding "no shortcuts" should rule out unless the operator explicitly defines its safety envelope.

#### Open Decisions (Operator Must Choose)

##### D1 ÔÇö Classification Trigger: when is something a "question"?

| Option | Composes existing | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|
| **D1a** Heuristic on input shape | New ~30-LOC classifier; starts with `what`/`why`/`how`/`when`/`where`/`who`, ends with `?`, no slash prefix | Cheap; brittle; false-positives ("what should we work on?" is a goal, not a Q) |
| **D1b** Extend `complexity_classifier.py` with `ComplexityClass.QUESTION` | Existing `ComplexityClass.TRIVIAL` + `fast_path_eligible` flag already extends naturally | Canonical; ~80 LOC; needs labeled examples for the classifier |
| **D1c** Explicit prefix `?<question>` or `/ask <question>` | New verb via existing `verb_registry` seam; zero ambiguity | Explicit + safe; requires operator habit change |

**RECOMMENDED**: D1c (`/ask` verb) for the first ship ÔÇö explicit-prefix is the only option that's binding-clean. Operators can adopt the habit voluntarily; the auto-classifiers (D1a / D1b) can ship later as additive paths if D1c proves load-bearing.

##### D2 ÔÇö Answer Authority: where does the answer come from?

| Option | Composes existing | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|
| **D2a** Lightweight Claude call (no tools, no thinking, low max_tokens) | `providers.ClaudeProvider` already supports thinking=off; just need a curated system prompt + max_tokens=400 + temperature=0.3 | Fast (~3-5s); ~$0.005/op; risks confabulation on project-specifics |
| **D2b** Semantic index lookup over docs + CLAUDE.md + commits | `semantic_index.py` already builds recency-weighted centroid; add cosine-top-K retrieval + render snippets | Cheapest (~$0/op, local); limited coverage (only indexed text); no synthesis |
| **D2c** Hybrid: try D2b first, fall back to D2a if confidence low | Both surfaces above + a confidence threshold | Best of both; needs threshold tuning; two failure modes |

**RECOMMENDED**: D2c (hybrid). The semantic index already exists and is being populated continuously ÔÇö leveraging it first is the binding-aligned path. The Claude fallback handles the long-tail. **Open**: the confidence threshold (cosine score) needs empirical tuning, which itself is deferred until traffic exists.

##### D3 ÔÇö Cost Contract: which budget pays?

| Option | Composes existing | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|
| **D3a** IMMEDIATE budget (existing) | `cost_governor.py` already tracks per-route spend | Simple; Q&A competes with operator-triggered urgent work |
| **D3b** New `INFORMATIONAL` sub-budget | New env knob `JARVIS_INFORMATIONAL_BUDGET_USD` + `urgency_router.Route.INFORMATIONAL`; separate ledger | Cleanest accounting; new route requires AST-pin update |
| **D3c** No additional budget ÔÇö Q&A free under the master cap | Existing cost_governor enforces; Q&A is "fee waived" if total spend below cap | Simplest; risks budget exhaustion via Q&A spam |

**RECOMMENDED**: D3b (separate sub-budget). Aligns with the manifesto ┬º5 urgency-route taxonomy. Operator sets the Q&A budget independently. ~50 LOC addition to `urgency_router` + `cost_governor`.

##### D4 ÔÇö Memory & Audit: what's recorded?

| Decision | Default | Composes existing |
|---|---|---|
| Q&A turns recorded in `ConversationBridge`? | **YES** (source=`ask_human_q`/`ask_human_a` variant) | Bridge already supports source tags |
| Q&A turns appear in postmortems? | **NO** (informational; not state-changing) | `last_session_summary.py` digest excludes them |
| Q&A turns count toward `LastSessionSummary` `attempted` stat? | **NO** (separate `questions_asked` counter) | Additive field on SessionRecord |
| Q&A persists to `long_horizon_memory` ledger? | **NO** (questions aren't state) | Ledger semantics preserved |

**These defaults are recommendations ÔÇö operator can flip any of them.** All four compose existing canonical surfaces; no new ledger needed.

##### D5 ÔÇö Failure Escalation: what if fast-path is wrong?

When fast-path Q&A returns a suspicious answer, operator must have an escape:

| Option | Composes existing | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|
| **D5a** Prefix re-trigger: `/ask --full <question>` re-runs through full pipeline | Existing `/ask` verb with `@arg_spec: [--full]` tag | Operator-controlled escape; cheap |
| **D5b** Auto-escalate when semantic-index confidence below `min_confidence` AND Claude fallback flags uncertainty | Composes D2c hybrid + Claude's confidence signals | Adaptive; risk of false-escalations |
| **D5c** Always cache + offer re-run: every Q&A surfaces `/expand q-N` to re-run through full pipeline | Existing `BoundedBodyStore` pattern (`t-N`/`d-N`/`o-N`/`n-N`/`p-N`) extends to `q-N` | Most operator-friendly; ~30 LOC addition |

**RECOMMENDED**: D5c (q-N refs). Aligns structurally with the existing artifact-ref family ÔÇö operator never loses access to the question, and re-running is one verb away. Composes the established `cross_substrate_ref` pattern.

#### Composition Map ÔÇö Existing Files to Compose (When Built)

| Capability | Canonical file |
|---|---|
| Verb registration (`/ask`) | `verb_registry` via `_handle_ask` on SerpentREPL |
| Question classification (D1b future) | `complexity_classifier.py` |
| Route assignment (D3b) | `urgency_router.py` |
| Provider call (D2a) | `providers.ClaudeProvider` (thinking=off variant) |
| Semantic retrieval (D2b) | `semantic_index.get_default_index()` |
| Cost tracking (D3b) | `cost_governor` + new env knob |
| Turn recording | `conversation_bridge.get_default_bridge()` |
| Audit ledger | `cross_process_jsonl` (┬º33.4 pattern) |
| Q&A ref family (D5c) | `BoundedBodyStore` pattern (mirror of `t-N`/`d-N`/`o-N`/`n-N`/`p-N`) |

**Every capability composes an existing canonical surface.** No parallel state. No new registry. Adding a new sub-budget to `urgency_router` is the only schema change, and it's additive (the closed-5-value Route taxonomy gains a 6th member, AST-pin updates atomically).

#### What's OFF the Table (Non-Decisions)

These are ruled out by the operator binding and **cannot ship under any path**:

1. **Q&A bypassing semantic_guardian / boundary_gate** ÔÇö if a question generates ANY mutation suggestion, it MUST route to full pipeline. Fast-path is read-only by definition.
2. **Q&A auto-triggering** from sensors / autonomous proposals ÔÇö only operator-typed input ever invokes fast-path. The 16 sensors continue to use full pipeline.
3. **Q&A as a shortcut for code questions** ÔÇö `where is X tested?` MUST route to Venom tool loop (search_code), not Q&A. The classifier (D1b future) must reject code-shape questions explicitly.
4. **Skipping conversation_bridge capture** ÔÇö even though Q&A turns are informational, they shape context for subsequent ops. The bridge captures them regardless.

#### Recommended Phased Engineering (When Operator Approves)

| Phase | Scope | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| **Phase 0** ÔÇö Substrate | New `_handle_ask` verb + `/ask` dispatch; D2a Claude-direct backend; D3a IMMEDIATE budget (cheapest first ship); D5c q-N refs | ~3 days |
| **Phase 1** ÔÇö Hybrid retrieval | Add D2b semantic_index lookup; D2c confidence-threshold fallback | ~5 days |
| **Phase 2** ÔÇö Budget isolation | D3b INFORMATIONAL route + sub-budget; AST-pin updates for closed-route taxonomy | ~3 days |
| **Phase 3** ÔÇö Classifier (optional) | D1b ComplexityClass.QUESTION; auto-route when prefix omitted | ~5 days |
| **Total when operator approves**: ~2 weeks across 4 phases | | |

**Until then, this section is the design-doc anchor.** Future-me / operator can return, pick paths, and engineering follows.

#### Open Empirical Questions (Cannot Decide Without Traffic)

1. **What's the actual Q&A volume?** Until operator regularly types questions, the cost-budget knob value is a guess.
2. **What's the semantic-index hit rate?** Depends on how well CLAUDE.md / PRD / commits cover question-shaped queries ÔÇö needs ÔëÑ50 question samples to tune.
3. **Does the explicit-prefix habit stick?** D1c (`/ask`) requires operator habit. If after 2 weeks no questions are typed, the prefix is friction; if 50+ questions are typed, D1b auto-classification becomes worth shipping.

These three answers come **after** Phase 0 ships and traffic accumulates ÔÇö they're Layer 3 empirical evidence (┬º41.6), not Layer 2 engineering.

---

What needs BUILDING (not just composing) for Tier B/C/D. Per-substrate composition surface noted so each is "thin composer" over canonical existing files.

| # | Capability | Status (post-2026-05-11 closure) | Effort | Composition surface | Commit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **RoadmapReader** substrate | Ô£à **SHIPPED 2026-05-11** (`roadmap_reader.py`) | ~1-2 weeks | Operator-signed `.jarvis/roadmap.yaml` ÔåÆ HMAC-SHA256 verify ÔåÆ parse ÔåÆ emit IntentEnvelopes via UnifiedIntakeRouter; closed RoadmapVerdict (NO_ROADMAP/VALID/INVALID_SIGNATURE/MALFORMED) + GoalPriority taxonomies | `d0eb3780b6` |

| 4 | **Multi-step plan orchestrator** | Ô£à **SHIPPED 2026-05-11** (`multi_step_orchestrator.py`) | ~2 weeks | Enforces DAG contract at RUNTIME (not just emit-time); phased dep-gated emission (only emits sub-goals whose deps reached COMPLETED); closed OrchestrationVerdict/SubGoalRunState; idempotent (already-EMITTED filtered from READY); bounded (max_emits_per_tick default 5) | `6cc1c52340` |

| 8 | **Infrastructure recovery loop** | Ô£à **SHIPPED 2026-05-11** (`infra_recovery_loop.py`) | ~1-2 weeks | Unified periodic scanner composing posture_health task-death classifier (Wave 1 ┬º37 Tier 1 #2) + worktree_manager.reap_orphans (Manifesto ┬º2) + 2 new detectors: stale-lock-with-dead-PID + summary-less-session-dir (post-Layer-8 forensic). **DUAL master/mutation gate per Manifesto ┬º6** ÔÇö `AUTO_RECLAIM_ENABLED` default-FALSE hard opt-in for any file mutation; bounded by MAX_RECOVERIES_PER_RUN (default 10); 3-tier PID liveness probe (psutil ÔåÆ os.kill(0) ÔåÆ ps -p subprocess) | `169fe5ceb0` |

**Cross-suite regression baseline post-closure: 825 tests across all 9 ┬º41.4 substrates** (171 ÔåÆ 253 ÔåÆ 334 ÔåÆ 426 ÔåÆ 502 ÔåÆ 608 ÔåÆ 721 ÔåÆ 825 as substrates accumulated). 61 AST pins + 86 FlagRegistry seeds + 9 new SSE events registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES`.

**Each substrate confirmed compliant with the canonical pattern**: ┬º33.1 default-FALSE master flag, AST-pinned authority asymmetry (forbids orchestrator/iron_gate/policy/providers/candidate_generator/urgency_router/change_engine/semantic_guardian/auto_committer/risk_tier_floor/tool_executor/plan_generator imports), frozen ┬º33.5 artifacts with to_dict + schema_version stamp, lazy-imported composers (NEVER duplicating logic). No parallel state, no hardcoding ÔÇö same discipline as ┬º40 Wave 1-3 ships.

**What this unlocks per ┬º41.8 phased roadmap**:

* **Phase 2 (Tier B operational) prerequisites complete** ÔÇö RoadmapReader (#1) + Goal Decomposition Planner (#2) are the named blockers for "Tier B operational" in ┬º41.8's Phase 2 row.
* **Tier C quality-floor scaffolding shipped** ÔÇö Mutation Testing Harness (#5) + Coverage Gate (#6) + Architectural Taste Layer (#3) raise the test/quality floor required for autonomous operation without per-PR operator review.
* **Cross-session resilience triad shipped** ÔÇö Long-Horizon Memory (#7) + Infrastructure Recovery Loop (#8) + Multi-Day Deadlock Detector (#9) enable continuous unsupervised days ÔÇö the Layer 3 evidence threshold gating Tier D readiness.

### ┬º41.5 NEW Operator-Flagged Gap ÔÇö Web Search & Browsing Capability  **[SUBSTRATE + VENOM WIRING SHIPPED 2026-05-11]**

**Closure status**: Substrate shipped `31933e5da9` (`web_browser.py` 1580 LOC, 74 substrate tests). Venom tool-loop wiring shipped `16152a29ef` (3 new tools registered in `_L1_MANIFESTS` ÔÇö `web_browse(url, js_render)` / `web_follow(url)` / `web_cite(url, fragment)` ÔÇö routing through `perform_browsing_action` with `BrowsingAction` + `BrowsingVerdict` closed taxonomies; legacy `web_fetch`/`web_search` left byte-identical per augment-not-replace; master `JARVIS_WEB_BROWSER_ENABLED` ┬º33.1 default-FALSE for operator-paced graduation; 20 wiring regression tests + 5 AST pins on dispatch source). Cumulative ┬º41.5 surface: **94 tests** across substrate + wiring. The remainder of this subsection is preserved as the original design intent record.

---



The operator flagged this directly: *"I don't think web search and being able to browse the web like how Claude does is integrated in O+V."* They are correct. This is a load-bearing gap for autonomous JARVIS development.

**Current state (Venom tool loop, per CLAUDE.md)**:

* `web_fetch(url)` ÔÇö fetches a single URL, returns response body as text
* `web_search(query)` ÔÇö runs a search query, returns top results as plain text

**What Claude has that O+V doesn't**:

| Capability | Claude | O+V today |
|---|---|---|
| Single-URL fetch | Ô£à | Ô£à `web_fetch` |
| Search-as-text | Ô£à | Ô£à `web_search` |
| Multi-page navigation (follow links) | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| JavaScript-rendered content | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| Image extraction from pages | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| PDF parsing from web sources | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| Authenticated browsing (cookies/sessions) | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| Search-then-browse-then-cite chains | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| Citation tracking across operations | Ô£à | ÔØî |
| Per-domain trust boundary | Ô£à (implicit) | ÔØî |

**Why this is load-bearing for Tier D autonomous JARVIS development**:

* **Researching new libraries / APIs** ÔÇö when M10 ArchitectureProposer wants to integrate a new substrate (e.g., a new vector DB, a new MCP server), it needs to *read the library's documentation* + *understand version compatibility* + *check for security advisories*. `web_fetch` + `web_search` aren't enough ÔÇö needs link-following.
* **Reading docs for features being built** ÔÇö autonomous development of a feature requires reading docs in the form they're published (often JS-heavy, multi-page).
* **Following PR discussions** ÔÇö when proposing changes that touch external dependencies, reading the upstream project's PR discussions for context.
* **Investigating bug reports** ÔÇö autonomous debugging requires reading external issue trackers (GitHub Issues, Stack Overflow, etc.).
* **Citing sources** ÔÇö Tier D autonomous commits should reference external sources for justification. The cage's adversarial autobiography (┬º40 Wave 1 #8) gets stronger when citations are auditable.

**Proposed substrate: `web_browser.py`**

* New canonical substrate ~3-5 weeks effort
* Composes existing:
    * `tool_executor.py` Venom tool loop (canonical tool dispatch)
    * `cross_process_jsonl.py` flock'd ledger (citation history)
    * `governance_boundary_gate.py` (URL allowlist by domain ÔÇö operator-tunable via `JARVIS_WEB_BROWSER_DOMAIN_ALLOWLIST`)
    * `conversation_bridge.redact_secrets` (Tier -1 sanitization of fetched content for credential leaks)
    * `mcp_output_scanner.py` (the Wave 3 #5 scanner ÔÇö reused for browser output scanning)
* Closed taxonomies:
    * `BrowsingAction` enum (NAVIGATE / FOLLOW_LINK / EXTRACT_TEXT / EXTRACT_IMAGE / SEARCH / CITE)
    * `BrowsingVerdict` enum (CLEAN / CREDENTIAL_LEAKED / OUT_OF_ALLOWLIST / RATE_LIMITED / FAILED)
* New Venom tools registered:
    * `web_browse(url)` ÔÇö multi-step navigation with link discovery
    * `web_follow(link_id)` ÔÇö follow a discovered link
    * `web_cite(url, fragment)` ÔÇö record a citation to the JSONL ledger
    * Replaces or augments existing `web_fetch` / `web_search`
* AST pins:
    * Master default-FALSE per ┬º33.1 (cognitive substrate awaiting graduation)
    * Authority asymmetry (NO orchestrator/iron_gate/policy imports)
    * Composes Wave 2 #5 boundary gate (no parallel URL allowlist)
    * Composes Wave 3 #5 MCP scanner (no parallel credential redaction)
    * Closed `BrowsingAction` + `BrowsingVerdict` taxonomies bytes-pinned
* ┬º33.4 audit ledger at `.jarvis/web_browsing_ledger.jsonl` (every navigation + citation recorded)
* SSE event `web_browsing_action` registered in canonical broker
* IDE GET route `/observability/web-browsing` for operator-visible browsing history

**Why this is in ┬º41 rather than ┬º40**:

┬º40 Waves 4-5 were calibration learning + experimental (item-numbered #9-#22). Web browsing is a *prerequisite capability* for autonomous JARVIS development ÔÇö it doesn't fit cleanly into "calibration." ┬º41 promotes it to a load-bearing capability gap for Tier D.

**Sequencing**: Web browser substrate ships in **Phase 0** of ┬º41.8 roadmap (alongside UX Polish Slices 2-3, ~3-5 weeks).

### ┬º41.6 Layer 3 ÔÇö Empirical Evidence Requirements (Wall-Clock)

What CANNOT be engineered around ÔÇö months of operator-paced cadence accumulating evidence that the cage holds in production.

| Evidence threshold | Current | Tier A min | Tier B min | Tier C min | Tier D min |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| M10 proposals shipped with no regressions | 0 | 1 | 10 | 30 | 100 |
| OV-signed commits adversarially audited clean | 2 | 10 | 30 | 50 | 200 |
| Cadence flags graduated default-TRUE | 1 | 5 | 10 | 20 | 30 |
| Antivenom gaps closed | 0 of 5 found | 5 | 10 | 20 | 50 |
| Continuous unsupervised days | 0 | 1 | 7 | 30 | 90 |
| Coherence ratio sustained across N sessions | 1.000 on 3 | 1.000 on 10 | ÔëÑ0.95 on 30 | ÔëÑ0.90 on 50 | ÔëÑ0.85 on 100 |
| Postmortem-fusion lessons retained | 0 (substrate missing) | 0 | 5 | 20 | 50 |
| Cross-session belief-revision cycles | 0 | 0 | 5 | 20 | 50 |

**Wall-clock timeline at current cadence (1 soak / 8h cron, ~3/day)**:

* Tier A min: ~2 weeks
* Tier B min: ~3 months
* Tier C min: ~6 months
* Tier D min: ~12-18 months

The cadence engine is structurally bulletproof (Layer 6/7/8 closed). What's missing is **wall-clock time** + **autonomous activity volume**. The cron + soak harness handles the wall-clock; M10 master flag flip + roadmap consumption handle the activity volume.

---

### ┬º41.7 Layer 4 ÔÇö The Architectural Decision (Tier C ÔåÆ Tier D)

This is the hard one. Tier C ÔåÆ Tier D isn't an engineering problem ÔÇö it's a design decision about what "safe" means when operator isn't in the per-PR loop.

The cage's safety properties (Wave 2 boundary gate, hash-cap, M10 forced approval, protected branches) all *assume operator-in-the-loop* as a structural invariant. Removing operator requires choosing a **replacement authority** that the cage can compose against.

Four options, ordered by cost-effectiveness:

**Option 1 ÔÇö Signed Roadmap Document (RECOMMENDED)**

* Operator writes `.jarvis/roadmap.signed.yaml` quarterly + signs it cryptographically (operator's GPG key)
* Roadmap declares: scope (which subsystems O+V may modify), budget (cost cap + time cap), success criteria (test coverage, posture stability, drift bounds)
* New substrate `signed_roadmap.py` verifies signature + parses scope/budget
* Boundary gate + hash-cap consult roadmap: changes WITHIN scope auto-approve; OUT-OF-scope route to approval_required (same as today)
* RoadmapReader (Layer 2) consumes the signed roadmap directly
* Operator's authority retained: quarterly review of the roadmap document, not per-PR review
* **Tradeoff**: operator still does work, just at a slower cadence; substantially less than per-PR review
* **Effort**: ~2-3 weeks substrate + ongoing operator effort (quarterly)

**Option 2 ÔÇö Multi-Operator Quorum**

* 2-of-3 maintainers must co-sign architectural commits
* New substrate `quorum_authority.py` collects + verifies signatures
* Replaces operator-singleton-authority with operator-quorum-authority
* **Tradeoff**: requires recruiting 2 additional humans willing to act as quorum signers
* **Effort**: ~4-6 weeks substrate + 2 additional humans + signature-collection workflow

**Option 3 ÔÇö External Trusted Oracle**

* CI/CD pipeline (e.g., GitHub Actions w/ required human reviews on critical paths) replaces in-process operator review
* Composes existing GitHub API + governance_boundary_gate for which paths require review
* **Tradeoff**: still has human in critical-path loop (just outside the local repo); needs CI infrastructure
* **Effort**: ~3-4 weeks substrate + CI configuration

**Option 4 ÔÇö Self-Bounding (Research-Grade)**

* O+V proves to itself that a change is safe (proof carriers, formal verification, type-theoretic guarantees)
* Composes AlphaVerus-style formal verification + Constitutional Classifiers
* **Tradeoff**: currently unproven approach; research-grade only
* **Effort**: 6-12 months R&D; high risk of not working

**Recommendation**: **Option 1 (Signed Roadmap Document)**

* Lowest engineering cost
* Retains operator authority at the long-horizon level (quarterly signing) without per-PR friction
* Composes naturally with existing Wave 2 substrates (boundary gate consults roadmap)
* Compatible with Tier D operation while preserving the zero-order-doll architecture
* Doesn't require recruiting additional humans (Option 2) or research breakthroughs (Option 4)

**This decision should be made explicitly before Phase 3 (Tier C operational) ÔÇö earlier is better since the chosen path constrains substrate design.**

---

### ┬º41.8 Roadmap to Tier D ÔÇö Phased 18-30 Month Plan

| Phase | Duration | Milestone | Engineering | Operator |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Phase 0** | Today ÔåÆ 2 weeks | UX polish + WebBrowser substrate | Ship Slice 2 + Slice 3 + `web_browser.py` substrate | Review weekly |
| **Phase 1** | 2 wks ÔåÆ 3 months | **Tier A operational** | M10 master flag flip + first 10 proposals + cadence-flag graduations | Review weekly, approve PRs |
| **Phase 2** | 3 ÔåÆ 6 months | **Tier B operational** | RoadmapReader + goal decomposition planner | Review monthly, batch-approve aligned proposals |
| **Phase 3** | 6 ÔåÆ 12 months | **Tier C operational** | Architectural taste + mutation testing + infra recovery + Layer 4 decision | Review quarterly, dashboard overseer |
| **Phase 4** | 12 ÔåÆ 18 months | **Layer 4 substrate** | Build chosen replacement authority (recommended: signed_roadmap.py) | Sign roadmap quarterly |
| **Phase 5** | 18 ÔåÆ 30 months | **Tier D operational** | Sustained evidence accumulation; cage proves Tier D-safe empirically | Sign roadmap; receive monthly status report |

**Total realistic timeline to Tier D from 2026-05-11**: **18-30 months**.

Most of the timeline is **Layer 3 wall-clock evidence + Layer 4 architectural decision**, not engineering. The engineering portion is ~14-22 weeks (~4-5 months) focused work.

**Decision gates between phases**:

* **Phase 0 ÔåÆ Phase 1**: UX polish is at parity; WebBrowser substrate has shipped + Phase 9 cadence has flipped ÔëÑ5 cadence flags.
* **Phase 1 ÔåÆ Phase 2**: ÔëÑ10 M10 proposals shipped clean; ÔëÑ30 OV-signed commits adversarially audited clean.
* **Phase 2 ÔåÆ Phase 3**: RoadmapReader is consuming a signed roadmap (even an unsigned one initially); goal decomposition is producing multi-step plans.
* **Phase 3 ÔåÆ Phase 4**: Layer 4 decision is made + substrate has been designed (even if not yet shipped).
* **Phase 4 ÔåÆ Phase 5**: Signed roadmap substrate is operational; first quarter of operator-paced quarterly review cycle complete.

What to build NOW vs. what to defer, by time horizon.

**This week (Phase 0)**:

1. **UX Polish Slice 2** (welcome banner + tutorial + did-you-mean) ÔÇö closes 6 of 19 remaining UX gaps; ~1-2 weeks
2. **UX Polish Slice 3** (tab completion + fast-path + slash palette) ÔÇö closes 7 more UX gaps; ~1-2 weeks
3. **WebBrowser substrate** ÔÇö unblocks autonomous research capability; ~3-5 weeks

**This month (Phase 1 prep)**:

4. **M10 master flag flip** ÔÇö first autonomous architectural proposal; operator reviews + accepts/rejects ÔåÆ graduation contract begins accumulating evidence
5. **Phase 9 cadence cron** ÔÇö already running at 8h interval; just needs to keep running

**3 months (Phase 2)**:

6. **RoadmapReader substrate** ÔÇö operator writes `.jarvis/roadmap.yaml` (unsigned initially); sensors consume goal items
7. **Goal decomposition planner** ÔÇö above PLAN phase; takes roadmap goal ÔåÆ decomposes into op chain
8. **Slice 4** ÔÇö resumable conversations + conversation export + search-in-conversation

**6 months (Phase 3)**:

**12 months (Phase 4)**:

**18+ months (Phase 5)**:

17. **Trinity expansion** ÔÇö once Tier D is stable, J-Prime and Reactor Core repos can be brought online with the same cage discipline

### ┬º41.10 Honest Framing & Decision Points

**The ┬º40 ÔåÆ ┬º41 reframe**:

* ┬º40 Waves 1-3 were framed as "advanced forward roadmap." They closed RRD recursion-bounding, adversarial cage completion, cross-session coherence, polish substrates. Each Wave shipped clean.
* ┬º41 reframes those Waves as **engineering preamble to Tier D**, not destination. The Tier D framing was implicit; ┬º41 makes it explicit.
* This isn't moving the goalposts ÔÇö it's making them visible.

**The fundamental decision the operator faces**:

* **Path A: Stop at Tier B (monthly review)** ÔÇö Achievable in 6-9 months. ~70% of "fully autonomous" value with ~10% of the architectural risk. Operator's role becomes batch-approver of weekly-batched proposals. Most pragmatic.
* **Path B: Push to Tier D (roadmap-level only)** ÔÇö Achievable in 18-30 months. Requires Layer 4 architectural decision. Fully realizes the operator-binding stated goal. Highest value, highest cost, highest architectural risk.
* **Path C: Recognize Tier D isn't the goal you actually want** ÔÇö The operator-in-the-loop pattern is O+V's load-bearing safety property. Other "fully autonomous" agents fail because they have no equivalent cage. Tier B might be the sweet spot architecturally.

**The operator binding 2026-05-11 confirms Path B is the destination**. ┬º41 is the honest decomposition of that path.

**┬º41 vs. ┬º40**: ┬º40 closure was structural completion of the recursion-bounding triad + UX polish + cross-session coherence. ┬º41 is what comes after ÔÇö the actual destination. Future sessions reference ┬º41.8 (the phased roadmap) for sequencing decisions; reference ┬º41.7 (the architectural decision) for the load-bearing architectural choice; reference ┬º41.5 (web browsing) for the operator-flagged capability gap; reference ┬º41.6 (empirical evidence) for the wall-clock reality check.

**The cage doesn't get weaker as we move toward Tier D ÔÇö it gets stronger**. Boundary gate stays; hash-cap stays; antivenom stays; M10 forced-approval stays (but consults signed roadmap for scope). The operator's authority migrates from per-PR to roadmap-level. That's the actual architecture.

**┬º41 status**: roadmap shipped 2026-05-11. Phase 0 work (Slice 2 + Slice 3 + WebBrowser substrate) is the next operator-paced sequence.

---

### ┬º41.11 Empirical Claims Audit & Open Verification Tickets (NEW 2026-05-14)

**Origin.** During post-submission audit of the OV research paper ┬º92 (Recent Developments addendum), the SICA-pattern empirical-demonstration claim in ┬º92.16 thread (1) + ┬º92.18 was found to overstate what `git log --author=Claude` actually supports. Specifically: the paper claimed "On 2026-05-11, O+V autonomously shipped two self-modification commits (Posture Aurora + Karen voice command router)" ÔÇö but both commits are dated **2026-05-10** and **authored by Derek J. Russell** (no Claude signature, no Co-Authored-By trailer, no AutoCommitter attribution). The 23 commits actually authored `Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>` in the repo are **all** appends to `docs/governance/passb_graduation_ledger.jsonl` ÔÇö autonomous bookkeeping under the Phase 9 cadence, **not** governance-code self-modification in the SICA-paper-strict sense.

**Correction commits.** Research paper ┬º92.16 + ┬º92.18 corrected and pushed to `origin/main` as **`ec07677c4a`** on 2026-05-14. The "demonstrated three years ahead of 2028" framing was reframed from *retroactive demonstration* to *structural readiness on a multi-month timeline well inside Anthropic's 2028 ASL-4 horizon*. The five-thread closure count in the ┬º92.16 summary was honestly downgraded from 3-of-5 to 2-of-5 (Treefinement L2 + SWE-Bench-Pro harness binding remain real citationÔåÆimplementation closures; SICA / Constitutional / ASL-4 are positioned-but-not-empirically-closed).

**Why this section exists.** Two reasons: (i) make the empirical gaps **engineering tickets** with graduation criteria, not just paper caveats ÔÇö every overstated claim becomes a concrete ladder item the cadence can climb; (ii) preserve the audit trail for any future reviewer who walks the ┬º92 corrections back to the PRD. The tickets below are the load-bearing items that, once closed, fully retire the ┬º92.16 caveats ÔÇö at which point the "demonstrated SICA pattern" framing becomes *actually demonstrated*, not *positioned to demonstrate*.

#### ┬º41.11.1 SICA-pattern empirical demonstration ÔÇö fully-autonomous governance self-modification

**The claim that needs proving.** O+V autonomously generates, validates, and commits a code mutation to a *governance file* (`iron_gate.py`, `semantic_guardian.py`, `policy_engine.py`, `risk_tier_floor.py`, `auto_committer.py`, etc.) under an autonomous signature, surviving the full Iron Gate Ôêÿ Semantic Guardian Ôêÿ TestRunner Ôêÿ AutoCommitter cycle with **zero operator intervention** during the generateÔåÆvalidateÔåÆcommit window.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- Ô£à Autonomous-ledger-write: 23 `Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>` commits to `docs/governance/passb_graduation_ledger.jsonl` (verifiable: `git log --author=Claude --diff-filter=A`).
- Ô£à NOTIFY_APPLY-mediated code generation with operator-approved commit (substrate-complete; AutoCommitter constructs the signature, operator approves the diff overlay).
- Ô£à AutoCommitter primitive end-to-end: `auto_committer.py` constructs commits with O+V signature, conventional commit format, risk-tier metadata, protected-branch push prevention.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ÔØî Fully-autonomous *generate ÔåÆ validate ÔåÆ commit* cycle without operator intervention.
- ÔØî Self-modification of governance code (only application code or ledger files have been auto-committed historically; governance files are still human-authored).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** **ÔëÑ1 commit visible in `git log --author=Claude --diff-filter=M` against a governance file**, accompanied by:

1. Iron Gate AST validation green (no immutable-rule violation logged)
2. Semantic Guardian inspection green (no hard finding)
3. TestRunner green for the scoped test set
4. AutoCommitter signature present (Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer + O+V signature line)
5. Commit message conforms to conventional format with risk-tier metadata
6. **Zero operator approval events** in the audit ledger for the op span

Repeat across **3 consecutive clean soaks** under Phase 9 cadence ÔÇö at that point graduate.

**Prerequisites.**

- ┬º41.11.4 AutoCommitter graduation-**evidence gate** returns `READY` (NOT "flag flips default-TRUE" ÔÇö the flag is already default-TRUE; the real prerequisite is the gate's per-soak-window Yellow-tier O+V evidence verdict, see ┬º41.11.4 premise-correction)
- `risk_tier_floor` configuration permitting Yellow-tier unattended-apply on governance ops (currently floor is `notify_apply` or stricter for `ORDER_2_GOVERNANCE` per Pass B)
- Pass B Order-2 governance cage (┬º40.1) graduated default-true OR the SICA candidate's scope kept below Order-2 (Body-only governance ops only)

**Tier placement.** ┬º41.8 Phase 1 candidate (months 6ÔÇô12 of the 18ÔÇô30 month roadmap). Single highest-leverage empirical milestone ÔÇö closing this ticket alone retires ~40% of the ┬º92.16 thread (1) caveat.

#### ┬º41.11.2 Anti-Venom parity-scale evaluation ÔÇö Constitutional Classifiers benchmark

**The claim that needs proving.** Anti-Venom's deterministic substrate achieves **Ôëñ4.4% catastrophic-error rate** against an adversarial corpus of comparable scale to Anthropic's Constitutional Classifiers benchmark (*arXiv:2501.18837* ÔÇö 86%ÔåÆ4.4% reduction on Claude 3.5 Sonnet).

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- Ô£à Adversarial Cage Phase 9.4: **0/38 escapes** on hand-authored adversarial corpus (12/38 *documented* gaps ÔÇö predicted detection-gaps, not active escapes).
- Ô£à Semantic Firewall 11 injection detectors + 5 credential shapes + recursion ban + ScopedToolBackend mutation cage (┬º92.1).
- Ô£à Iron Gate 12 immutable rules + Semantic Guardian 10 pre-apply detectors.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ÔØî Parity-scale corpus (Constitutional Classifiers uses thousands of synthetic jailbreaks; current Adversarial Cage is 38 hand-authored).
- ÔØî LLM-generated adversarial mutations (the ┬º40.1 #3 "Anti-Venom self-immunization" tier ÔÇö generate jailbreaks to harden the cage).
- ÔØî Real Claude-class generative core under the cage during adversarial evaluation (current 0/38 was deterministic-only ÔÇö no live LLM in the loop).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** Adversarial corpus expanded to **ÔëÑ3,000 inputs** (ÔëÑ100├ù the current 38), drawn from:

1. LLM-generated adversarial mutations (┬º40.1 #3, gated by JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_SELF_IMMUNIZATION_ENABLED)
2. Curated jailbreak corpus from public benchmarks (HarmBench-style + Constitutional Classifiers replications)
3. Domain-specific O+V-targeted attacks (governance-mutation attempts, recursion attempts, credential exfiltration shapes)

Acceptance gate: **Ôëñ4.4% catastrophic-error rate** (matching Anthropic's benchmark) across the full ÔëÑ3,000-input corpus, with 12/38 ÔåÆ Ôëñ132/3000 documented-gap inflation acceptable.

**Prerequisites.**

- Ô£à **┬º40.1 #3 Anti-Venom self-immunization tier ÔÇö SHIPPED 2026-05-14** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/self_immunization.py`, ~900 LOC + 47-test spine + 6 AST pins + 5 FlagRegistry seeds). The deterministic mutation corpus generator. Closed 3-taxonomy substrate (`MutationStrategy` 8-value / `ImmunizationVerdict` 4-value / `ImmunizationOutcome` 4-value), 8 pure structural mutation operators derived from the documented-gap evasion taxonomy (alias-rebind / dunder-reconstruct / getattr-indirect / string-split / whitespace-pad / comment-decoy / unicode-confusable / identity-control), async bounded-concurrency campaign runner composing the canonical `adversarial_cage.evaluate_entry` (detection single-source-of-truth ÔÇö zero re-implementation), `cross_process_jsonl.flock_append_line` ledger, `_process_singletons.get_semaphore` concurrency, `MutationProvider`/`HardeningSink` Protocol DI. Master flag `JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_SELF_IMMUNIZATION_ENABLED` ┬º33.1 default-FALSE. AST-pinned authority asymmetry (no orchestrator/iron_gate/policy_engine/change_engine/candidate_generator imports). `summarize_campaign()` exposes the `meets_parity_gate` boolean read against `JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_TARGET_ESCAPE_RATE` (default 0.044). **The "no hardcoding" property holds**: parity-scale corpus volume is produced dynamically (`seeds ├ù strategies`), not enumerated by hand.
- AdversarialReviewer subagent extended for full-corpus parallel evaluation (currently scoped per-op, not corpus-batch) ÔÇö *remaining*
- LLM-driven `MutationProvider` implementation wired (the deterministic 8 operators ship; the optional LLM-augmentation Protocol seam exists but no concrete provider is wired yet) ÔÇö *remaining*
- Test-time compute budget: ~$50ÔÇô200 per parity-scale evaluation pass (DW Tier 0 estimated) ÔÇö *remaining (only once LLM MutationProvider is wired; the deterministic path is zero-LLM-cost)*

Closing this ticket retires the ┬º92.16 thread (3) caveat (Constitutional Classifiers comparison stops being "structural analog" and becomes "empirical parity result"). **The substrate prerequisite is now CLOSED** ÔÇö the remaining work is corpus *scale* (ÔëÑ3,000 inputs via wired LLM MutationProvider + AdversarialReviewer corpus-batch extension) and the empirical parity soak, not the generator itself.

#### ┬º41.11.3 ASL-4 recursion-bound stability under sustained autonomy stress

**The claim that needs proving.** Under sustained self-modification activity (i.e., ┬º41.11.1 graduated and firing regularly), the ┬º1 Boundary recursion-depth gate (┬º40.1 #1) + hash-cap on self-modification (┬º40.1 #2) **hold for ÔëÑ7 cumulative days of unattended-apply autonomy** without any boundary violation logged.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- Ô£à Recursion-depth gate is implemented in `boundary_recursion_gate.py` (per ┬º40 Wave 2 #5 closure, 2026-05-10).
- Ô£à Hash-cap on self-modification proposals exists structurally (┬º40.1 #2).
- Ô£à Unit-tested invariants: gate refuses recursion-depth > N; hash-cap refuses identical-hash mutations within window.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ÔØî Stability *under stress* ÔÇö gate must hold during sustained autonomous self-modification, not just during synthetic test pressure.
- ÔØî 7-day continuous operation under autonomy (longest unattended soak to date is hours, not days).
- ÔØî Recovery behavior when gate fires under real autonomy (synthetic recovery tested; live-fire recovery has never happened because the gate has never fired in production).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).**

- **ÔëÑ7 days cumulative** of unattended-apply autonomy with ┬º41.11.1 firing (so the gate has actual self-modification activity to constrain, not just idle uptime).
- **Zero ┬º1 Boundary violations** logged in the audit ledger across the 7-day span.
- **At least one synthetic stress-test** where the gate's recursion-depth threshold is artificially lowered to fire under load, and the recovery path (operator notification + safe-state retreat) runs end-to-end.

**Prerequisites.**

- ┬º41.11.1 must graduate first (no stress test until self-mods are happening ÔÇö the gate must have actual pressure to demonstrate stability against)
- 7-day unattended-apply session feasibility (current battle-test harness wall-clock cap is hours; needs longer-soak infrastructure or a sequence of stitched-together soaks with state-preservation)
- Synthetic stress-test fixture (one-shot, gated by a dev-only env flag, fires gate intentionally and verifies recovery)

**Tier placement.** ┬º41.8 Phase 2 candidate (months 12ÔÇô18, after ┬º41.11.1 and concurrent with ┬º41.11.2). Closing this ticket retires the ┬º92.16 thread (4) caveat (ASL-4 framework alignment stops being "structural mapping" and becomes "empirical stability result").

#### ┬º41.11.4 AutoCommitter graduation-evidence gate for unattended apply

**PREMISE CORRECTION (2026-05-14 ÔÇö a ┬º92.16-class self-audit catch).** The original framing of this ticket said *"AutoCommitter's `JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED` flag graduates from `default-FALSE` (current) to `default-TRUE`."* **That premise was false** and was an unverified claim authored into this PRD by the same hand that authored ┬º92.16. `auto_committer.py` line 83 reads `os.environ.get("JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED", "true")` ÔÇö **the flag is and was already `default-TRUE`.** "Flip the flag" was never the work. This is exactly the class of overclaim ┬º41.11 exists to retire, caught here in the PRD itself by the same forensic discipline (`git`/source verification, not assertion).

**The claim that actually needs proving (reframed).** The generic `GraduationLedger.is_eligible()` proves *"soaks were clean"* ÔÇö it does **not** prove *"AutoCommitter actually fired on a real Yellow-tier (`NOTIFY_APPLY`) op during those clean soaks."* A soak is clean even if every op was `SAFE_AUTO`, or no op fired, or all commits were human-authored. Graduating AutoCommitter (or asserting ┬º41.11.1's SICA prerequisite is met) on clean-soak-count alone is a ┬º92.16-class overclaim. What needs proving is **per-soak-window evidence** that AutoCommitter exercised the unattended-apply path.

**SHIPPED 2026-05-14 ÔÇö the AutoCommitter graduation-evidence gate** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/auto_commit_graduation_gate.py`, ~700 LOC + 33-test spine + 6 AST pins + 4 FlagRegistry seeds). Closed taxonomies `CommitEvidenceKind` (3) + `AutoCommitEvidenceVerdict` (5: READY / LEDGER_NOT_ELIGIBLE / EVIDENCE_INSUFFICIENT / NO_GIT_HISTORY / MASTER_OFF). **Composes** the canonical `GraduationLedger` clean-soak counter + `_read_all` row reader (never re-implements it) and **adds** the missing evidence layer: genuine per-soak-window `git log` attribution (consecutive clean-`SessionRecord.recorded_at_epoch` define each window; oldest soak bounded by `JARVIS_AUTOCOMMIT_GRAD_LOOKBACK_DAYS`, caveat stated in the diagnostic ÔÇö *claim exactly what the evidence supports*). A soak has evidence iff ÔëÑ1 commit in its window carries **both** the canonical O+V signature (derived from `auto_committer.ov_signature_substring()` ÔÇö not hardcoded) **and** the Yellow marker (derived as `f"Risk: {RiskTier.NOTIFY_APPLY.name}"` ÔÇö not hardcoded). `READY` only when the generic ledger is eligible **and** every counted clean soak has window evidence; otherwise `EVIDENCE_INSUFFICIENT` with the bare soaks named. git access via `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` argv-list (no shell, repo standard, AST-pinned). Master `JARVIS_AUTOCOMMIT_GRADUATION_GATE_ENABLED` ┬º33.1 default-FALSE. Authority-asymmetry AST-pinned (no orchestrator/iron_gate/policy_engine/change_engine/candidate_generator). The gate **NEVER flips the flag** ÔÇö it makes the operator's evidence-driven decision auditable; `evidence_summary().meets_evidence_gate` is the single boolean a REPL/SSE surface reads.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- Ô£à `JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED` is already `default-TRUE` (verified `auto_committer.py:83`).
- Ô£à AutoCommitter primitive end-to-end (commit signature, conventional format, risk-tier metadata, protected-branch push prevention).
- Ô£à The graduation-evidence gate substrate (this ship) ÔÇö deterministic, fail-closed, 33 tests + 6 AST pins green.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ÔØî The empirical *evidence* itself ÔÇö i.e. the gate returning `READY` against a real ledger + real git history (requires actual Phase 9 clean soaks where AutoCommitter fired on Yellow-tier ops). The gate is the *instrument*; the soak is the *measurement*. Fabricating the measurement would be the ┬º92.16 mistake ÔÇö not done.
- ÔØî Unattended-apply on governance ops (still requires `notify_apply` floor or stricter; that floor relaxation is a separate operator decision the gate informs but does not make).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** `evaluate_graduation_evidence()` returns `AutoCommitEvidenceVerdict.READY` against the live ledger + live git history ÔÇö meaning generic clean-soak eligibility **and** ÔëÑ1 Yellow-tier O+V commit in every counted clean-soak window. That verdict (not clean-soak-count, not operator judgment) is the evidence-driven gate for any unattended-apply scope decision.

**Prerequisites.**

- Phase 9 cadence runs producing clean soaks where AutoCommitter fires on real Yellow-tier ops (the measurement the instrument reads ÔÇö operator-paced, cannot be fabricated).
- ┬º41.11.2 Anti-Venom parity-scale eval *should ideally close first* (soft prerequisite ÔÇö don't widen unattended autonomy before the cage is parity-verified).

**Tier placement.** ┬º41.8 Phase 1 candidate (months 6ÔÇô12). The **substrate prerequisite is now CLOSED** ÔÇö ┬º41.11.1's SICA demonstration can no longer be claimed on clean-soak-count alone; it must pass this gate's `READY` verdict, which structurally prevents the ┬º92.16-class overclaim at the graduation boundary.

#### ┬º41.11.5 Reverse Russian Doll bounded-RSI formal proof (dissertation cross-reference)

**The claim that needs proving.** The Reverse Russian Doll architecture (┬º3, ┬º50) is a *sufficient condition* for bounded recursive self-improvement, formalizable as a thermodynamic stability theorem on the (*C*, *S<sub>n</sub>*) system under the Wang RSI formulation (*arXiv:1805.06610*) extended to non-stationary, multi-repository, memory-augmented settings.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- Ô£à Conjecture sketch (research paper ┬º92.17, claims a/b/c).
- Ô£à Three Wang RSI extensions identified (non-stationary kernel / coupled multi-chain state / memory-augmented capability score).
- Ô£à Thermodynamic-entropy formulation sketched (shell as deterministic-entropy reservoir; core as thermal source; AST-pin invariants as lower bound on shell entropy).

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated (and is not engineering work ÔÇö this is dissertation work).**

- ÔØî Formal proof of (a) monotone non-decreasing deterministic-entropy of *S<sub>n</sub>*.
- ÔØî Formal proof of (b) monotone non-increasing conditional semantic-entropy of *C* under *S<sub>n</sub>*.
- ÔØî Formal proof of (c) net agentic entropy bounded above by a finite constant.
- ÔØî Empirical validation of the proof against O+V's actual Phase 9 graduation-cadence data.

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** Peer-reviewed publication (or arXiv pre-print + faculty endorsement) of the **bounded-RSI theorem** under the working dissertation title ÔÇö *"Thermodynamic Containment of Agentic Entropy: Formalizing the Reverse Russian Doll Architecture for Bounded Recursive Self-Improvement"*. Acceptance criteria match standard ML-conference / safety-research review (NeurIPS / ICLR / SaTML / Anthropic-aligned venue).

**Prerequisites.**

- Penn MSE-AI program enrollment (in-progress; UPenn application submitted 2026-05-13).
- Dissertation work under Eric Wong (certifiably robust networks) / Aaron Roth (trustworthy-learning) / PRECISE Center lineage.
- ┬º41.11.1 ÔÇö ┬º41.11.4 empirical closures provide the *substrate evidence* the proof validates against.

**Tier placement.** ┬º41.8 Phase 3 (months 18ÔÇô30) ÔÇö dissertation-track, not engineering-track. This ticket exists in the PRD as a **placeholder + cross-reference**, not an engineering deliverable. The engineering arc closes at ┬º41.11.1 ÔÇö ┬º41.11.4; the formal arc opens with ┬º41.11.5 and is the dissertation work proper.

Both callers: if seam returns PlanResult, use it; if None (master-off OR non-actionable consensus OR ESCALATE), fall through to existing single-shot `generate_plan` path. **Zero behavior change at default** (all 4 master flags FALSE). **Action recommendation routing**: ACCEPT_CANONICAL ÔåÆ use consensus winner; CLAMP_TO_NOTIFY_APPLY ÔåÆ use winner (risk-tier clamp is downstream GATE phase's concern); ESCALATE_TO_OPERATOR_REVIEW ÔåÆ fall through to single-shot (operator reviews via SSE event + recorded verdict; pipeline continues); FALL_THROUGH ÔåÆ fall through (disabled/failed). **Master-flag AND-composition** (no parallel flag ÔÇö uses substrate's internal `evaluate_dispatch_decision` check): `JARVIS_MULTI_PRIOR_PLANNING_ENABLED` (Slice 1 materializer) AND `JARVIS_MULTI_PRIOR_RUNNER_ENABLED` (Slice 2 runner) AND `JARVIS_MULTI_PRIOR_DISPATCH_ENABLED` (Slice 3 dispatch); `JARVIS_MULTI_PRIOR_OBSERVER_ENABLED` (Slice 4 ledger) gated internally by `record_dispatch_outcome`. **Route + posture gates** (substrate-enforced via `materialize_priors`): `route=complex` AND `posture=EXPLORE` ÔÇö naturally narrow blast radius; only the ops that most benefit trigger multi-prior. **Authority asymmetry (AST-pinned via test)**: Seam imports stdlib + `verification/multi_prior_*` + `plan_generator` only. NEVER imports `orchestrator` / `iron_gate` / `policy_engine` / `candidate_generator` / `providers` / `change_engine` / `semantic_guardian` ÔÇö pure integration adapter, zero policy. **15 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_multi_prior_plan_seam.py`: master-off + partial-flags returns None (3 tests); substrate-gate-fails returns None (route + posture, 2 tests); exception isolation NEVER raises into caller (1 test); **8 AST pins** (canonical substrate imports / authority asymmetry / rehydration via `_parse_plan_response` / coherence via `_validate_plan_coherence` / `record_dispatch_outcome(verdict)` invoked / `Prior.system_prompt_addendum` threaded / `seed` threaded / two-caller consumer presence in plan_runner.py + orchestrator.py); schema version canonical literal (1 test). **273 broader Move 6.5 regression tests still green** (all 6 substrate slices unaffected ÔÇö Slice 1 through Slice 6). **Why this matters for RSI** (operator binding clarification #6): graduation observations accumulate ONLY via `record_dispatch_outcome` ÔÇö pre-v2.97 the helper was unused so Slice 6's graduation contract (`is_ready_for_graduation()` needs 50+ observations) could never advance. Post-v2.97, when operator flips the 3 substrate master flags via Phase 9 cadence, every PLAN-phase op on (complex, EXPLORE) routes through multi-prior dispatch + records to ledger. ### ┬º43 Resource-Zero Watchdog -- Severing the Kill Path From the Guarded System *(NEW 2026-05-16 -- postmortem bt-2026-05-17-024509)*

**Root cause.** The wall-clock hard-deadline thread (`_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread._watch` in `harness.py`) was a watchdog that *shared resources with the system it guarded*. Session `bt-2026-05-17-024509` proved both legacy escalation layers poisonable:

- **Old Layer 3** sent `SIGTERM` to itself and trusted the harness asyncio signal handler to write a partial summary. Signal delivery was queued behind the same starved interpreter, so the *external* SIGTERM was swallowed -- proven wedgeable.
- **Old Layer 4** emitted a `logging`-module diagnostic and called `_atexit_fallback_write` before `os._exit(75)`. Both acquire the logging lock. A wedged producer held that lock, so the watchdog's own kill path blocked on lock acquisition and never reached the exit. The logging lock *was* the poison.

A watchdog that shares the signal path **and** the logging lock with the system it guards is not a watchdog.

**The fix -- resource-zero collapse.** Layers 3 and 4 are *deleted, not tuned*. Composed within the existing Layer-7 dual-clock skeleton (monotonic + wall anchors untouched), the kill path now touches **nothing a poisoned process can hold**:

1. A raw `os.dup(2)` panic fd captured at *arm time* (pre-wedge), stored as `self._wd_panic_fd`.
2. `os.write` to that fd for every diagnostic -- the startup announce too, never `logging` (a poisoned lock at arm time would deadlock the thread *before* its tripwire is established; `try/except` cannot rescue a blocked acquire).
3. `os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGKILL)` -- never SIGTERM, never the asyncio signal handler, never `call_soon_threadsafe`. `signal` is the module-level import (resident in `sys.modules` before the thread ran) so not even the import lock is touched on the last line of defense.
4. `os._exit(137)` (128 + SIGKILL) as the absolute backstop.

The hard-kill tripwire (`time.monotonic() >= hard_kill_monotonic or time.time() >= hard_kill_wall`) is the **first statement** in the watchdog while-loop, before any poisonable call. The bounded grace still gives the clean asyncio `summary.json` path its full window; only a wedge past the extra margin trips the unblockable SIGKILL. `debug.log` remains the canonical session record per CLAUDE.md -- a partial `summary.json` is not worth a poisonable blocker on the last line of defense.

**Retired config.** `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_ESCALATION_SIGTERM_S` / `..._EXIT_S` tuned the deleted poison path and are retired (named only in a provenance comment -- a no-op knob misleads operators). The resource-zero grace stays operator-tunable and bounded via `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_HARD_KILL_MARGIN_S` (floor 5s / ceiling 300s).

**Spine.** `tests/battle_test/test_phase_d_resource_zero_watchdog.py` -- 8 AST pins enforce the structural invariants (panic fd dup'd at arm time / no `logging` anywhere on `_watch` / no SIGTERM-or-signal-handler / no `_atexit_fallback_write` / raw `write -> kill -> exit` triplet ordered / tripwire-first / dead knobs retired / margin env-bounded) plus a subprocess behavioral proof that the shipped function under a wedged loop terminates with `returncode -9` and emits its `RESOURCE-ZERO HARD KILL` diagnostic on the pre-dup'd fd. The AST pins are load-bearing: a future edit re-introducing `logging`/`SIGTERM`/`_atexit_fallback_write` onto the path would silently re-open the exact poison vector. Postmortem follow-up: a headless soak with `OUROBOROS_BATTLE_MAX_WALL_SECONDS` set proves the kill path under real wedge conditions.
