**Version (prior)**: 2.96 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Layer 8 closure ÔÇö atexit-fallback marker discrimination (harness-intended vs external)**: Closes Layer 8 root cause exposed by live-fire soak `bt-2026-05-10-221432`. **Diagnosis**: the 3rd Phase 9 graduation soak fired v2.92 Layer 7 dual-clock watchdog correctly (skew=210s host sleep detected, cap fired on wall-clock authority at effective=2404s), v2.88 Layer 6 atexit fallback wrote summary.json before ShutdownWatchdog os._exit(75) fired at 35.7s ÔÇö **all 3 prior layers held structurally**. But the resulting history row classified as `outcome=infra` because the partial summary stamped `session_outcome=incomplete_kill` (causeÔåÆoutcome adapter conflated harness-intended terminations with external interruptions) AND the soak classifier's `_SHUTDOWN_NOISE_STOP_REASONS` set included `wall_clock_cap`. **Root cause** ÔÇö TWO compounding design errors: (1) `termination_hook_default_adapters._CAUSE_TO_SESSION_OUTCOME` mapped WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED ÔåÆ `incomplete_kill` (the original Slice 3 design rationale from 2026-05-07 said "every cause that was previously classified as incomplete_kill MUST still map to incomplete_kill" ÔÇö but that pre-dated CLAUDE.md's clean-bar-equivalence footnote which states wall_clock_cap is equivalent to idle_timeout); (2) Slice 7c (2026-05-07) added `wall_clock_cap` to the soak classifier's noise set as a defensive move BEFORE v2.88 Layer 6 atexit fallback shipped ÔÇö post-v2.88, the composite `wall_clock_cap+atexit_fallback` is a graceful-shutdown signature, NOT an external-boundary-cut-us-off signature. The adapter contradicted CLAUDE.md; the classifier conflated harness-intended with external. **Architectural insight**: there are TWO distinct termination classes ÔÇö **external-signal causes** (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP ÔÇö the harness did NOT intend this termination ÔåÆ INFRA correct) and **harness-intended causes** (WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED ÔÇö the harness's own watchdogs/gates fired per design ÔåÆ CLEAN correct). Pre-Layer-8 conflated both under "incomplete_kill" + noise. Layer 8 separates them at the source. **Structural fix ÔÇö 2 seams at source**: (1) `termination_hook_default_adapters.py:153-163` ÔÇö WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED now stamp `"complete"` (was `"incomplete_kill"`); external-signal causes unchanged; NORMAL_EXIT still None; UNKNOWN still incomplete_kill (safe default). (2) `live_fire_soak.py:380-400` ÔÇö `wall_clock_cap` REMOVED from `_SHUTDOWN_NOISE_STOP_REASONS` (was 5 entries; now 4 entries ÔÇö sigterm/sighup/sigint/harness_idle_timeout; symmetric with idle_timeout which has always been clean per CLAUDE.md). **Why source-discrimination (not downstream filtering)**: alternative considered (special-case branch in classify_outcome for `wall_clock_cap+atexit_fallback`) ÔÇö rejected because it treats a symptom not the root cause, AND because LastSessionSummary parser + audit tooling + future graduation arcs would each need the same filter (duplication). Source-discrimination fixes the marker once; every downstream consumer sees the canonical value. **16 new regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer8_atexit_marker_discrimination.py`: adapter mapping (6 ÔÇö WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDEDÔåÆcomplete; SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUPÔåÆincomplete_kill preserved; NORMAL_EXITÔåÆNone; UNKNOWNÔåÆincomplete_kill safe default) + classifier noise set (2 ÔÇö wall_clock_cap NOT in set; external signals still in) + forward-fix integration (1 ÔÇö bt-2026-05-10-221432 post-Layer-8 signature classifies CLEAN) + backward-compat (1 ÔÇö legacy incomplete_kill data still routes infra; Layer 8 fixes source not predicate) + **4 AST pins** (adapter cites Layer 8 + v2.96 / classifier cites Layer 8 + v2.96 / WALL_CLOCK_CAPÔåÆ"complete" bytes-pinned via ast.Dict walk / noise set size=4). **Existing tests updated** (no regressions): `test_termination_hook_slice3_wiring.py` (24 tests ÔÇö 3 expectation flips + commentary updates); `test_phase_9_slice_7c_composite_stop_reason.py` (32 tests ÔÇö 2 parametrize flips + canonical set assertion update + 2 new tests added: `_layer8_routes_clean` + `_uncomposed_wall_clock_cap_routes_clean`; renamed legacy test to `_legacy_incomplete_kill_routes_infra` to preserve backward-compat semantics). **100 broader regression green** across Layer 6 + Layer 7 + Layer 8 + Slice 3 wiring + Slice 7c composite. **Zero regression** on the cadence-arc spine. **Cadence arc state ÔÇö Layers 1-8 ALL CLOSED**: v2.86 Layers 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio) / v2.87 Layer 5 (modality ledger always loads) / v2.88 Layer 6 (Layer 4 escape-hatch writes partial summary) / v2.92 Layer 7 (dual-clock authority ÔÇö sleep/suspend immunity) / **v2.96 Layer 8 (atexit-fallback marker discrimination)**. The watchdog + atexit fallback + soak classifier now correctly classify wall_clock_cap as a clean harness-intended termination under ALL paths (clean shutdown completes / clean shutdown wedges + atexit fallback fires). Soak `bt-2026-05-10-221432` (the diagnostic session) STILL classifies as infra historically ÔÇö Layer 8 fixes the source not historical rows. The NEXT soak under Layer 8 will produce a correctly-classified clean row when wall_clock_cap fires under sleep. **Why this matters for RSI**: graduation soaks under Phase 9 cadence MUST produce reliable evidence rows. When the watchdog's stated contract ("kill after N seconds") is honored AND CLAUDE.md's stated semantics ("wall_clock_cap is clean-bar-equivalent") are honored, the soak's terminal classification mirrors the harness's intent. Pre-Layer-8 the system had a self-contradiction between code and documentation; Layer 8 resolves it in favor of the documentation (the authoritative source). Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: solved root problem directly (fixed marker at source where stamped, NOT downstream classifier ÔÇö avoids symptom-treatment; every consumer benefits); no workarounds (did NOT add special-case branch in classify_outcome; fix is structural via 2 canonical seams); no shortcuts (16 new tests + 4 AST pins + 56 existing tests updated; bytes-pin via ast.Dict walk); composes existing canonical paths (TerminationCause 8-value taxonomy unchanged, `_CAUSE_TO_SESSION_OUTCOME` dict changed 3 values, `_SHUTDOWN_NOISE_STOP_REASONS` frozenset removed 1 value, CLAUDE.md clean-bar-equivalence footnote now actually enforced); no hardcoding (semantic classes still derived from TerminationCause enum, no new strings invented); no duplication (2 seam edits; no parallel discrimination logic). **Files modified**: `backend/core/ouroboros/battle_test/termination_hook_default_adapters.py`; `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/graduation/live_fire_soak.py`; `tests/battle_test/test_termination_hook_slice3_wiring.py`; `tests/governance/test_phase_9_slice_7c_composite_stop_reason.py`; `tests/battle_test/test_layer8_atexit_marker_discrimination.py` (NEW). **NEXT** (operator-paced): run `--once` for soak #4 ÔÇö first graduation-eligible soak under the FULLY-closed cadence arc (Layers 1-8 all in place). If clean, `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` graduates default-FALSE ÔåÆ default-TRUE (first Phase 9 flag graduation under the fully-closed cadence arc). See v2.95 below for Venom V2 Slice 6 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.93 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Venom V2 Slice 4 SHIPPED ÔÇö IDE GET `/observability/tool-permissions` endpoint suite**: Forward-additive on v2.89 substrate + v2.90 REPL + v2.91 SSE. Completes the ┬º8 absolute-observability triad for Venom permission decisions: **ring (history) + REPL (operator query) + SSE (real-time push) + GET (browseable HTTP surface)**. **Three new routes** registered on the canonical `IDEObservabilityRouter`: `GET /observability/tool-permissions[?limit=N]` (recent N decisions, default 20 max 200) / `GET /observability/tool-permissions/by-tool/{tool_name}[?limit=N]` (exact-match filter) / `GET /observability/tool-permissions/{op_id}[?limit=N]` (exact-match filter, default 100). **Route-order discipline (AST-pinned via byte-index comparison)** ÔÇö `/by-tool/{tool_name}` MUST register BEFORE `/{op_id}`; aiohttp matches in registration order and the generic op_id pattern would otherwise capture the literal string `by-tool` as an op_id value. The pin `test_route_order_specific_before_parameterized` asserts source-code positional invariant via `src.index(by_tool_path) < src.index(by_op_path)`. **Dual master-flag gating** (no parallel knob): `JARVIS_IDE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED` (graduated default-TRUE 2026-04-20) ÔåÆ 403 when off (port-scanner discipline); `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` (default-FALSE per ┬º33.1) ÔåÆ 403 when off. Composed via new `_permission_archive_master_enabled()` static helper that defers to canonical `permission_archive_enabled()` ÔÇö mirrors `_posture_master_enabled()` pattern from v2.84. **Handler shapes mirror canonical posture-health handler** ÔÇö 403 on either master-off, 429 on rate limit, 400 on missing path parameter, 503 on substrate import failure (graceful degradation), 200 + canonical projection on happy path. **Query-param parsing** ÔÇö new `_parse_limit(request, default, ceiling)` static helper extracts `?limit=N` with bounds clamping (floor 1, env-tunable ceiling 200). Falls through to defaults on parse failure. NEVER raises. **Payload schema composes canonical projection** ÔÇö recent route returns `{schema_version, count, snapshot: {capacity/size/next_seq/utilization/schema_version}, records: [{ref, op_id, tool_name, decision_value, decision: {AggregatePermissionDecision projection with schema_version=tool_permission.1}, inserted_at, schema_version=permission_decision_archive.v1}]}`. IDE consumers correlate the event to `/expand p-N` retrieval via the canonical ref. **Surface field** in `/observability/health` extended with `tool-permissions` ÔÇö IDE clients feature-detect against comma-separated list. **Authority asymmetry preserved (AST-pinned)** ÔÇö IDE module imports ONLY `get_default_archive` + `permission_archive_enabled` from substrate. Does NOT import `BoundedDecisionArchive` (ring constructor), `DecisionRecord` (frozen dataclass), or `ArchiveSnapshot` (projection). Composes only `.to_dict()` projection contract ÔÇö preserves ┬º33.5 frozen-artifact discipline + forward-compat with future schema bumps. AST walker test verifies via `ast.ImportFrom` inspection (not source-string grep ÔÇö docstring mentions are fine). **15 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_ide_observability_tool_permissions.py`: route registration (3 ÔÇö all 3 paths registered / route-order specific-before-parameterized AST-pinned / surface field advertises tool-permissions) + gate enforcement (4 ÔÇö 403 when IDE off / 403 when archive off / 400 on missing tool_name / 400 on missing op_id) + happy path (6 ÔÇö empty archive 200+empty records / recent newest-first / limit query param respected / limit clamped to max / by_tool exact match / by_op exact match) + authority asymmetry (2 ÔÇö read-only contract handlers do not mutate archive / does not import policy substrate types). **Read-only contract** (load-bearing) ÔÇö `test_handlers_do_not_mutate_archive` records baseline `snapshot().to_dict()`, hits all 3 routes, asserts archive's snapshot is byte-identical post-call. Drift = operator-visible signal that someone added a mutating side effect. **129 broader regression green** across all Venom V2 slices + IDE observability. **Cumulative Venom V2 arc state (4 slices)**: v2.89 substrate (31 tests) / v2.90 REPL (31 tests) / v2.91 SSE (11 tests) / v2.93 GET (15 tests) = 88 new tests + 19 AST pins. **Concurrent soak #3** (`bt-2026-05-10-221432`) running while this slice shipped ÔÇö first soak under post-Layer-7 dual-clock watchdog (v2.92). When clean row lands, `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` graduates default-FALSE ÔåÆ default-TRUE (first Phase 9 flag graduation). **Files**: `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/ide_observability.py` (3 substantive blocks: register_routes / surface field / new helpers + handlers); `tests/governance/test_ide_observability_tool_permissions.py` (NEW, 15 tests + 2 AST pins). **Why this matters for RSI**: when O+V's proactive curiosity loop scales (e.g., the post-arc soak's "code_gen::.py import_error" intent firing real ops, not just BG-route no-ops), every ALLOW/DENY/ASK/DEFER decision becomes browseable in real time via the IDE extension. Operators can answer "what is the system choosing not to do" without log archaeology. This is the load-bearing pre-requisite for unattended autonomous-development cycles where the operator reviews Venom V2 decisions asynchronously. **NEXT** (operator-paced, each ~30min): Slice 5 FlagRegistry seed for `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_SIZE` env vars; Slice 6 PRD ┬º37 Tier 2 #6 row flip + ┬º3.6.3 row ­ƒƒí ÔåÆ Ô£à. **Version (prior)**: 2.92 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Layer 7 closure ÔÇö WallClockWatchdog dual-clock authority (sleep/suspend immunity) + Soak #2 evidence row landed**: Closes Layer 7 root cause exposed by live-fire soak `bt-2026-05-10-093428`. **Soak #2 result (2nd Phase 9 evidence row)**: `outcome=clean ops=16 cost=$0.0000 stop_reason=idle_timeout duration_s=39838 (~11h actual wall-clock ÔÇö see Layer 7 below)`. **Phase 9 ladder progress**: `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` now at **2 of 3 clean rows**. One more soak ÔåÆ flag graduates default-FALSE ÔåÆ default-TRUE. **v2.86ÔÇôv2.88 cadence-arc verified under second live-fire run**: Layer 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio) held; Layer 5 (modality ledger) held; Layer 6 (watchdog Layer 4 escape-hatch summary write) held ÔÇö `summary.json` written cleanly before `ShutdownWatchdog os._exit(75)` fired at terminal; `session_id` linked correctly in history row; soak harness produced parseable evidence. v2.89-v2.91 Venom V2 stack not exercised during BG-only soak (no STANDARD route ops escalated to permission evaluator). **Layer 7 root cause** (newly diagnosed during forensics): the soak ran 11h instead of its 40-min cap because `time.monotonic()` on macOS is backed by `mach_absolute_time()`, which **pauses while the CPU is halted** (host sleep/suspend). The laptop slept ~10.5h between 02:55 PDT and 13:36 PDT; monotonic advanced 1892s (~31.5 min); wall-clock advanced 39798s (~11h 03min); the cap (2400s monotonic) was never reached. Heartbeat trail: tick=48 at 10:59 PDT showed elapsed=1603s; tick=60 at 13:36 PDT showed elapsed=1790s ÔÇö only 187s monotonic advance over 2h 36min wall-clock = 2% clock efficiency. The hard-deadline thread (Slice B safety net from 2026-05-03 Defect #1 fix) uses the same `time.monotonic()` so it was paused identically. The watchdog's stated contract is "wall-clock cap" (cf. `_monitor_wall_clock` docstring: "Opaque ceiling on total session duration ... wall-clock elapsed time") ÔÇö monotonic-only enforcement violates that contract whenever the host suspends. **Structural fix at `harness.py`**: composes BOTH `time.monotonic()` AND `time.time()` anchors at task/thread entry; takes `max(elapsed_monotonic, elapsed_wall)` as effective elapsed. **NTP-rollback-safety preserved** (wall jumps backward ÔåÆ wall < monotonic ÔåÆ max picks monotonic). **Forward NTP jump handled** (max picks wall ÔÇö fires cap earlier than intended; acceptable for soak semantics where operator wanted "kill after N seconds of real time"). **Sleep/suspend handled** (monotonic paused ÔåÆ max picks wall ÔåÆ cap fires correctly on wake or earlier if wall already exceeded cap during sleep). Both `_monitor_wall_clock` (asyncio path) AND `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread` (Slice B thread path) modified; thread uses `min(remaining_monotonic, remaining_wall)` so whichever clock approaches the deadline first wins. Diagnostic log line in the thread's fire path includes `won_clock` field for operator-visible forensics. **Env-tunable skew warning** `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_SKEW_WARN_THRESHOLD_S` (default 60s, floor 5s, ceiling 3600s) ÔÇö when `elapsed_wall - elapsed_monotonic` exceeds threshold, log a debounced warning ("clock skew detected: monotonic=Ns wall=Ms skew=Ks (host sleep/suspend OR forward NTP jump). Treating wall-clock as cap-authoritative."). Debounce ensures sustained sleep doesn't spam the log every tick; re-warns only after another threshold-worth of additional drift. Cancel-path diagnostic upgraded: when async monitor cancelled (peer waiter wins FIRST_COMPLETED race), "NEVER fired" log line now reports BOTH monotonic and wall elapsed ÔÇö load-bearing for retrospective sleep-event forensics. **Canonical AST invariant updated** (`register_shipped_invariants`, harness.py:5194-5310): added `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_SKEW_WARN_THRESHOLD_S` to `REQUIRED_LITERALS` ÔÇö guarantees the skew-warn path doesn't regress to monotonic-only enforcement. AST walker now also detects `time.monotonic()` AND `time.time()` Attribute calls inside `_monitor_wall_clock` body ÔÇö violations raised if either is missing. **10 regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer7_dual_clock_watchdog.py`: 3 monitor-side pins (calls time.monotonic / calls time.time / composes via max()) + 2 thread-side pins (anchors both clocks + computes both deadlines / uses min() over remainings) + 1 env-knob pin (skew threshold referenced) + 1 canonical-validator green + **3 provenance pins** (cites v2.92 / cites bt-2026-05-10-093428 / cites mach_absolute_time). **Why no mocked functional test**: mocking `time.monotonic` AND `time.time` simultaneously interferes with `asyncio.sleep`'s internal scheduling (which uses `time.monotonic` for delay accounting). The event loop hangs. AST pins are the load-bearing guarantee ÔÇö drift in dual-clock composition is structurally detected; the `max(elapsed_monotonic, elapsed_wall)` literal is byte-pinned in source. **Test results**: 10 Layer 7 tests + 6 Layer 6 tests + 63 broader watchdog/wall-clock/partial-shutdown/termination-hook tests = **79 green, zero regression**. **Cadence arc state (Layers 1ÔÇô7 all CLOSED)**: v2.86 Layers 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio); v2.87 Layer 5 (modality ledger always loads); v2.88 Layer 6 (Layer 4 escape-hatch writes partial summary); **v2.92 Layer 7 (dual-clock authority ÔÇö sleep/suspend immunity)**. The watchdog now honors its "wall-clock cap" contract under ALL clock-skew scenarios (NTP step forward, NTP step backward, NTP slew, host sleep/suspend, host hibernate, virtualization-induced clock drift). **Why this matters for RSI**: graduation soaks run under operator-paced cadence. If the operator's laptop sleeps mid-soak, the watchdog MUST still enforce the cap so the soak doesn't run 11 hours and burn budget on idle ops. Layer 7 makes the cadence robust to host-side environmental conditions outside the harness's direct control ÔÇö the load-bearing pre-requisite for unattended Phase 9 graduation cycles. Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: solved root problem directly (composed both clocks at the seam where elapsed is computed; preserved monotonic's NTP-safety AND added wall's sleep-detection); no workarounds (did NOT add a "wake detector" that retroactively adjusts elapsed; did NOT switch entirely to wall-clock ÔÇö would lose NTP safety; composition via max() is the canonical resolution); no shortcuts (6 AST pins + 3 provenance pins + 1 canonical validator update; drift to single-clock structurally caught); composes existing canonical paths (existing periodic-check loop preserved from Defect #1 2026-05-03; existing hard-deadline thread structure preserved; existing skew-warn env-knob pattern mirrors `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_CHECK_INTERVAL_S` and `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_HARD_DEADLINE_GRACE_S`); no hardcoding (skew threshold env-tunable; existing knobs unchanged); no duplication (single seam at `_monitor_wall_clock` + single seam at `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread`; thread mirrors asyncio path exactly). **Files modified**: `backend/core/ouroboros/battle_test/harness.py` (3 substantive blocks: `_monitor_wall_clock` ~line 4561 dual-clock anchors + max-based effective_elapsed + skew warning + dual-clock diagnostics; `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread` ~line 4347 dual-clock anchors + dual deadlines + min(remaining) + dual-clock fire log; `register_shipped_invariants` ~line 5194 skew-threshold required-literal + dual-clock AST validation); `tests/battle_test/test_layer7_dual_clock_watchdog.py` (NEW, 10 regression tests). **NEXT** (operator-paced): (a) run `--once` for soak #3 ÔÇö final clean row to graduate `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` (Phase 9 first flag graduation under post-Layer-7 watchdog); (b) Venom V2 Slice 4 IDE GET `/observability/tool-permissions` (~1.5h); (c) Venom V2 Slice 5 FlagRegistry seed (~30min); (d) Venom V2 Slice 6 PRD ┬º37 Tier 2 #6 row flip (~30min). See v2.91 below for Venom V2 Slice 3 (SSE event) details.)

Comment block cites Slice 3 + PRD v2.91 + master-flag interaction. **Producer-bridge ┬º33.2** in `permission_decision_archive.maybe_record_decision`: after `archive.record()` returns a non-None record, attempts to publish the canonical `record.to_dict()` projection via the existing `publish_task_event(event_type, op_id, payload)` bridge ÔÇö composes the existing canonical bridge (ZERO parallel publish path, ZERO direct broker-coupling). **Positional invariant (AST-pinned)**: the publish call MUST come AFTER `archive.record()` ÔÇö load-bearing because publishing before would emit a stale ref / phantom record. **Defensive try/except (AST-pinned)**: broker exceptions MUST NOT propagate into the policy substrate; the policy path is fail-silent by contract. **Dual-master-flag composition (no parallel knob)**: archive master flag `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` (default-FALSE per ┬º33.1) gates `record()` first ÔåÆ archive-disabled means no record + no publish; stream master flag `JARVIS_IDE_STREAM_ENABLED` (graduated default-TRUE 2026-04-20 Gap #6 Slice 2) gates `publish_task_event` second ÔåÆ stream-disabled means record happens but publish is no-op. Both flags compose deterministically: `record_attempted = archive_enabled`, `publish_attempted = (archive_enabled AND record_succeeded)`, `publish_observed = (publish_attempted AND stream_enabled)`. **Payload schema** composes the canonical `DecisionRecord.to_dict()` projection: `ref` (`p-N`), `op_id` / `tool_name` / `decision_value` (allow/deny/ask/defer), `decision` (full `AggregatePermissionDecision.to_dict()` projection ÔÇö schema_version `tool_permission.1`, detail, deny_callbacks, ask_callbacks, total_callbacks), `inserted_at` / `schema_version` (`permission_decision_archive.v1`). IDE consumers can correlate the SSE event to a `/expand p-N` retrieval via the canonical ref. **11 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_permission_decision_sse.py`: 2 event-type registration pins (canonical literal value + presence in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset) + 4 producer-bridge contract tests (publish fires when both gates ON / silenced when stream OFF / silenced when archive OFF / payload carries canonical projection with deny + detail + schema_version) + 1 best-effort contract test (broker exception via mocked `publish_task_event.side_effect` MUST NOT propagate; archive write still succeeds) + **4 AST pins** (`EVENT_TYPE_PERMISSION_DECISION_RECORDED` constant present at module scope with canonical literal / constant appears in the `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset literal ÔÇö drift here is the same silent-drop failure mode v2.84 fixed / `maybe_record_decision` invokes `publish_task_event` AFTER `archive.record()` (positional invariant) / publish call wrapped in try/except (best-effort contract enforced structurally)). **137 broader regression green** across 5 files (`test_ide_observability_stream.py` + `test_section_37_tier1_2_posture_health_wiring.py` + `test_permission_decision_archive.py` Slice 1 + `test_tool_permissions_repl.py` Slice 2 + `test_permission_decision_sse.py` Slice 3) ÔÇö zero regression on the canonical broker substrate or on v2.84's POSTURE_OBSERVER_DEGRADED path (which is the canonical pattern v2.91 follows). **Cumulative Venom V2 arc state** (3 slices shipped tonight): Slice 1 (v2.89) substrate ring + producer-bridge + tool_executor seam wiring (31 tests); Slice 2 (v2.90) REPL verb auto-discovered + /expand p-N 5th cross-substrate prefix (31 tests); Slice 3 (v2.91) SSE event registration + producer-bridge to broker (11 tests); total 73 new regression tests + 16 AST pins across the 3 slices. **Why this matters for RSI**: the SSE event makes Venom permission decisions **observable in real time** ÔÇö when O+V's proactive curiosity loop fires tool calls at scale (e.g., the post-arc soak `bt-2026-05-10-082106`'s "code_gen::.py import_error" intent that fired 2026-05-10 02:43), operators see every ALLOW/DENY/ASK/DEFER event in the IDE stream as it lands. **Version (prior)**: 2.88 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Layer 6 closure ÔÇö wall-clock watchdog Layer 4 escape-hatch now writes partial summary.json before os._exit(75)**: Closes the final cadence-arc layer. **Diagnosis**: when the asyncio cleanup path wedged after wall-clock cap fires (provider streams stuck, slow `_shutdown_components`, etc.), the wall-clock watchdog escalated through 4 layers ending with `os._exit(75)` (line 4476). `os._exit` bypasses `atexit`, so Wave 3 v2.79's "partial-shutdown insurance" never fired ÔåÆ session dir contained only `debug.log` ÔåÆ `_read_most_recent_session` linkage failed ÔåÆ harness wrote `outcome=infra session=unknown` for every wall-clock-cap-induced soak (the operator-visible cadence pattern: 281/379 historical sessions had summary.json but the recent failure cohort had ZERO). Per-session audit confirmed: clean exits via SIGTERM (Layer 3) DID get atexit-fired summaries; only Layer 4-escalated exits had the gap. **Root cause**: the watchdog thread runs OUTSIDE the asyncio loop (specifically because the loop is wedged), so it has the unique capability to write sync I/O even when the rest of the harness is frozen. But it didn't take the opportunity. **Structural fix at `harness.py:4475-4504`** (just before `os._exit(75)` at line 4506): invoke the existing canonical `_atexit_fallback_write(session_outcome="incomplete_kill_layer4")` synchronously from the watchdog thread. The fallback is already designed for partial-shutdown contexts (Wave 3 v2.79: "this writer is pure-sync and defensive ... we can't trust the event loop or many imports") so it composes cleanly. New `incomplete_kill_layer4` outcome marker (distinct from Wave 3's `incomplete_kill` from Ticket B's signal-driven path) for operator-greppable audit. **Defensive try/except** wraps the fallback call so a fallback bug NEVER blocks the load-bearing escape hatch from firing ÔÇö `os._exit(75)` always runs even if `_atexit_fallback_write` raises. **Composes existing canonical primitives**: zero parallel summary writer, zero new state machinery, zero new env knob. The watchdog thread is already writing log lines via `_wd_log` and reading `self.<state>`; adding a sync method invocation is structurally additive. **6 regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer6_watchdog_layer4_summary.py`: (1) **AST positional pin** ÔÇö `_atexit_fallback_write` is invoked WITHIN the Layer 4 block AND BEFORE the `os._exit(75)` statement (load-bearing ÔÇö writing AFTER the kill is unreachable); regex anchored on `# ÔöÇÔöÇ Layer 4: os._exit` code marker (not the log-message string `"LAYER 4 ESCALATION..."`) + `try:\\s*os\\._exit\\(75\\)` actual statement; uses `block.rindex("os._exit(75)")` to find the executable call (not the substring inside the log message above the fallback). (2) **outcome marker pin** ÔÇö `incomplete_kill_layer4` literal must appear in the block (operator audit). (3) **defensive wrapping pin** ÔÇö fallback call must be inside try/except (a fallback bug must not block the escape hatch). (4) **functional integration** ÔÇö invoke `_atexit_fallback_write(session_outcome="incomplete_kill_layer4")` directly + assert summary.json gets written with the load-bearing `session_id` field populated (without it, soak-harness linkage falls back to `"unknown"` literal string). (5) **idempotence** ÔÇö when clean path already wrote summary, fallback is no-op (Layer 3 SIGTERM may trigger atexit which writes; Layer 4 must NOT clobber). (6) **provenance pin** ÔÇö Layer 4 block cites `Layer 6` + `v2.88` so future readers find the design doc. **Test results**: 6/6 + 25 wall-clock-cap + partial-shutdown tests still green (no regression). Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: solved root problem directly (the missing summary.json IS the link-failure cause, fixed at the source), no workarounds (we did NOT add a parallel summary path or move `os._exit` ÔÇö preserved the canonical escape hatch + composed the canonical fallback writer), no shortcuts (load-bearing positional invariant pinned via AST), composes existing `_atexit_fallback_write` substrate (Wave 3 v2.79), preserves operator-override discipline (no new env knob beyond what already exists). **Cadence arc COMPLETE**: with v2.86 + v2.87 + v2.88 in place, the live-fire soak harness produces parseable evidence even from wall-clock-cap-induced exits + the modality ledger correctly informs catalog ranking + the wrapper carries proxy/sentinel/.env semantics across all cadence paths. Next soak should produce its first cost-positive `outcome=clean` row when the operator chooses to run it. **Files modified in this slice**: `backend/core/ouroboros/battle_test/harness.py` (Layer 4 escape-hatch + fallback invocation, lines 4475-4504); `tests/battle_test/test_layer6_watchdog_layer4_summary.py` (NEW, 6 regression tests). See v2.87 below for Layer 5 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.87 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Layer 5 closure ÔÇö modality ledger now always loads (separated from verification probing)**: Closes the dominant cause of the 75% topology-blanket-block pattern observed in v2.86's verification soak. **Diagnosis** (deeper than v2.86): the topology sentinel's `lightonai/LightOnOCR-2-1B-bbox-soup` + `allenai/olmOCR-2-7B-1025-FP8` were correctly in TERMINAL_OPEN (403-deny + timeout terminal verdicts) but were nonetheless leading the BG/STANDARD candidate list at the sentinel dispatch site ÔÇö exhausted before the canonical `Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8` (CLOSED, healthy, accessible) could be tried. **Root cause**: `dw_discovery_runner._get_or_create_modality_ledger` at lines 592-611 conflated TWO orthogonal concerns under a single env flag (`JARVIS_DW_MODALITY_VERIFICATION_ENABLED`, default-FALSE): (a) loading the disk ledger of pre-classified verdicts (cheap pure-I/O ÔÇö should always be safe), and (b) probing new models live (expensive HTTP ÔÇö gated correctly). With the master flag off (default), the disk ledger's 22 pre-existing verdicts (14 CHAT_CAPABLE including Qwen3.5-397B + 8 UNKNOWN ÔÇö populated by prior verification cycles + persisted at `.jarvis/dw_modality_ledger.json`) were silently ignored. The classifier ran in legacy mode that ranked TERMINAL_OPEN models at the top. Sentinel dispatch exhausted them before reaching healthy candidates. **Structural fix** at `dw_discovery_runner.py:592-616`: separated the two concerns. Ledger loading is now unconditional (only short-circuited by explicit `JARVIS_DW_MODALITY_LEDGER_DISABLE=1` escape hatch for diagnostic isolation). Probing remains gated by the existing `modality_verification_enabled()` check at the canonical call site (`run_discovery` Step 1.5 lines 202-205) ÔÇö that gate is unchanged, so the expensive HTTP work still requires explicit operator opt-in. Verified via direct repro: `_get_or_create_modality_ledger()` returns a populated ledger with `has_record('Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8') == True`. **149 governance modality+discovery+catalog_classifier tests pass** with the fix in place ÔÇö no regression. Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: composes existing canonical primitives (zero parallel ledger machinery), separates conflated concerns (zero new env knob beyond an additive escape hatch), preserves operator-override discipline (explicit disable still works). **What v2.87 does NOT close** (Layer 6, pending v2.88): 0-cost session-id linkage failure. When the battle-test subprocess exits via wall-clock cap, `_read_most_recent_session`'s mtime-sort finds the session dir but `summary.json` isn't always written before SIGTERM/SIGKILL ÔÇö harness writes terminal row as `outcome=infra session=unknown`. The partial-shutdown insurance from Wave 3 v2.79 should cover this but apparently isn't firing in the wall-clock-cap exit path. Multi-hour investigation requiring instrumentation of atexit ordering + signal handler write paths + battle-test's own exit hook chain. **NEXT** (cadence arc remaining): Layer 6 deep-dive (atexit + signal handler write paths under wall-clock-cap exit) ÔÇö fresh-eyes session required. Once Layer 6 closes, the cadence should produce clean evidence at the first soak. See v2.86 below for the original 4-layer connectivity arc.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.86 (2026-05-10 ÔÇö **Cadence connectivity discipline ÔÇö 4 structural fixes for live-fire soak harness**: Closes the operator-paced cadence's silent-zero-cost / DW-blocked-by-topology runner-failure pattern that bit cron #1 on 2026-05-06 and every manual `--once` invocation since. **Discovery sequence** (5+ hour debug arc): operator launched first verification `--once` 2026-05-09 21:35 PDT; soak ran 30s then idle through wall-clock cap producing 0 ops + $0 cost (RUNNER classification). Iterative diagnosis peeled back **4 distinct structural layers**, each requiring its own fix; tonight's PRD entry captures the substrate work so the architecture is robust regardless of operator path (cron / launchd / `--once` / manual) and parent-process environment (Claude Code agent / IDE / terminal / CI). **Layer 1 ÔÇö `.env` not loading into harness subprocess**: canonical providers (`doubleword_provider.py:43-44`, `providers.py`) read `os.environ` at module load with no `load_dotenv()` call; the wrapper's env-block boundary is the canonical source-of-truth per the install script's design comment ("single source of truth for the env block") but it didn't source `.env`. **Fix at `scripts/run_live_fire_graduation_soak.sh:24-77`**: bash 3.2-portable `.env` loader using `case`/glob (not bash 4 regex ÔÇö macOS frozen at 3.2.57; `[!chars]` is literal in 3.2 so we use `[^chars]` POSIX form) + `eval "x=\${$key:-}"` indirect lookup (avoids the bash 4.2+ `${!var:-default}` combinator); validates KEY shape via two-stage `case` glob (`[A-Z_]*` head + `*[^A-Z0-9_]*` body negation). Operator-override discipline preserved: explicit shell-set values WIN over `.env`. **Layer 2 ÔÇö `--once` bypasses the wrapper's env-block**: `install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh::run_once` inlined a duplicate of the wrapper's env block, so the launchd path used the wrapper but `--once` did not. **Fix at `install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh:255-280`**: `run_once` now delegates to `WRAPPER_SCRIPT` (which the launchd plist already references at line 323) ÔÇö single env-block source of truth honored across all cadence paths; one inline duplicate eliminated. Cadence-kind enum bug caught (`once` ÔåÆ `adhoc` matching the wrapper's own preflight default). **Layer 3 ÔÇö `JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED` defaults to FALSE**: the YAML `brain_selection_policy.yaml` has `dw_allowed: false` for every route (intentional ÔÇö Phase 12 Slice E graduation purged YAML routing in favor of dynamic catalog from `dw_catalog_classifier`). The v2 dynamic-catalog path through `provider_topology.is_dw_blocked_for_route` is gated by THIS flag; without it, the v1 path reads YAML's `dw_allowed: false` and blocks every DW op. Catalog discovery completes correctly (`routes_assigned=['background','complex','speculative','standard']`) but the orchestrator never consults it because the sentinel flag is off ÔåÆ falls through to v1 yaml ÔåÆ all DW routes blocked. **Fix at `scripts/run_live_fire_graduation_soak.sh:104` (post-`.env`-load)**: `export JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED="${JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED:-true}"` ÔÇö operator-override discipline preserved (`=false` shell-set still wins). Verified end-to-end: `[CandidateGenerator] Phase 10 sentinel preflight: healthy=True schema=topology.2 routes_with_dw_models=['standard','complex','background','speculative']`. **Layer 4 ÔÇö `socksio` not installed ÔåÆ httpx 0.28+ ImportError on every Claude API call**: when the harness runs under a parent process whose network tunnel is `socks5h://localhost:N` (Claude Code agent sandboxing ÔÇö surfaced as `ALL_PROXY=socks5h://...` in the spawned subprocess env), httpx 0.28+ raises `Using SOCKS proxy, but the 'socksio' package is not installed. Make sure to install httpx using \`pip install httpx[socks]\`.` on every ClaudeProvider call ÔåÆ fallback path fails ÔåÆ ops stall waiting for either DW or Claude to recover. **First fix attempt (rejected as workaround)**: stripped proxy env vars in the wrapper. Caused `ClientConnectorDNSError: Cannot connect to host api.doubleword.ai:443 [nodename nor servname provided, or not known]` because the SOCKS proxy IS the network path under sandboxed agents ÔÇö stripping it broke DNS resolution. **Correct structural fix** ships in v2.86: `socksio==1.0.0` pinned in `requirements.txt:158` (next to `httpx==0.28.1` with provenance comment citing the sandbox-tunnel rationale). Wrapper preserves the inherited proxy block; httpx 0.28 now accepts the `socks5h://` scheme without raising. Verified end-to-end: `[ClaudeProvider] ÔåÆ stream model=claude-sonnet-4-6 timeout=196.0s max_tokens=16384 temp=1.0 thinking=on` ÔÇö Claude streams flow through the SOCKS5h tunnel cleanly. **What still doesn't work** (deeper than v2.86 closes ÔÇö pending v2.87 investigation arc): topology sentinel still **blanket-blocks DW under load** even with all 4 fixes ÔÇö when models hit transient failures (`/v1/files` ConnectionTimeoutError, heavy probe ConnectorTimeout on `moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6` / `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR-2`), the sentinel marks DW unhealthy and routes subsequent BG/STANDARD ops as `background_dw_blocked_by_topology` despite Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8 being reachable. Final 38-min soak stats: 24 ops processed, **18 (75%) blocked by topology** despite all 4 fixes; 0-cost outcome ÔåÆ `JARVIS_LIVE_FIRE_USE_GRADUATION_CONTRACT` correctly downgrades to RUNNER but the harness's terminal row writes `outcome=infra session=unknown` when `_read_most_recent_session` can't link the session because battle-test was killed before atexit could write `summary.json`. **Layer 5 (topology blanket-block) + Layer 6 (session-id linkage in 0-cost terminal path) require dedicated investigation** ÔÇö multi-component (heavy probe + topology sentinel + classifier + session-detection anchor) + hours of careful instrumentation. Honest pause point: 4 fixes shipped tonight are real ship-able structural improvements that strengthen the system permanently regardless of cadence outcome. **Test results**: cumulative regression spine still green across all v2.85-shipped surfaces (no new tests in v2.86 ÔÇö these are configuration/dependency fixes, not new substrate). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim across all 4 fixes ("solve the root problem directly ÔÇö without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). Composes existing wrapper script (single source of truth honored), existing install script delegation pattern (launchd + cron paths already used the wrapper), existing topology sentinel architecture (only the master-flag gate was missing), existing httpx[socks] extras pattern (`socksio` is the canonical opt-in dep ÔÇö already discovered by httpx, just needed installation). Zero new env knobs invented; zero parallel network paths; zero shell-portability hacks (the loader is bash-3.2 + zsh + bash-4+ portable). **NEXT** (cadence arc): Layer 5 + Layer 6 deep-dive ÔÇö the topology blanket-block under load + 0-cost session-linkage. Multi-hour investigation requiring instrumentation of heavy probe ÔåÆ modality ledger ÔåÆ catalog classifier ÔåÆ topology sentinel ÔåÆ orchestrator chain. Until those close, the cadence will produce RUNNER-attributed evidence even with v2.86's 4 fixes in place; the substrate's clean baseline at session start (`socks=0 dns=0 blocked=0`) proves the structural fixes work; the under-load behavior is the deeper layer. See v2.85 below for ┬º35 row #4 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.85 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #4 SHIPPED ÔÇö Cross-runner artifact contract (schema-versioned)**: Closes ┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #4 / ┬º3.6.3 priority #4 ("Cross-runner artifact contract"). **Last engineering arc** before the autonomy roadmap transitions to operator-paced Phase 9 cadence. Maps to fragility vector #8. **Root problem**: Wave 2 PhaseRunner extraction threads 10 cross-phase artifacts (`generation`, `episodic_memory`, `generate_retries_remaining`, `advisory`, `best_candidate`, `best_validation`, `t_apply`, `risk_tier`, `consciousness_bridge`, `cancel_token`) via untyped `ctx.artifacts` Mapping. Verbatim extraction sidestepped the issue. As soon as a runner is *refactored* beyond verbatim, one unversioned dict shape change (rename / type drift / phase-ownership confusion) crashes the FSM mid-pipeline with no recovery path. **Closure** ÔÇö single substrate file `governance/artifact_contract.py` (~530 LOC pure-stdlib, op_context-only import for OperationPhase): closed 10-value `ArtifactKind` taxonomy mirroring `phase_dispatcher.PhaseContext` slots 1:1 (AST-pinned coverage); closed 6-value `ValidationOutcome` taxonomy (OK / UNKNOWN_KEY / TYPE_MISMATCH / WRONG_PRODUCER / SCHEMA_VERSION_SKEW / PASSED_DISABLED); frozen `ArtifactSpec` dataclass with `kind` + `key` + `producer_phases` (FrozenSet) + `consumer_phases` (FrozenSet) + `validate_value` (duck-typed callable ÔÇö avoids importing producer types) + `schema_version` (per-artifact, e.g., `"generation.1"`); bytes-pinned `_ARTIFACT_REGISTRY` tuple of 10 `ArtifactSpec` entries; pure-function `validate_artifact_value(key, value, producer_phase)` returning frozen `ArtifactValidation` (NEVER raises ÔÇö failures surface via outcome enum); `validate_artifacts_bundle` for the dispatcher's iteration; `first_failure` helper for fail-fast strict mode. **Wiring at SINGLE choke point** `phase_dispatcher.py:828` ÔÇö composes canonical validator BEFORE `pctx.merge_artifacts(...)` so unknown keys are rejected before they reach `extras` (where they'd be silently lost forever). Master flag `JARVIS_ARTIFACT_CONTRACT_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ┬º33.1; `JARVIS_ARTIFACT_CONTRACT_STRICTNESS` selects `advisory` (default ÔÇö log only) or `strict` (raise `PhaseContextError` on first failure). **Catches 4 violation classes**: (1) UNKNOWN_KEY ÔÇö rename / typo / new artifact lacking spec entry (the #1 most common refactor crash class); (2) TYPE_MISMATCH ÔÇö value fails per-artifact `validate_value` predicate (e.g., `t_apply` receives a string instead of float); (3) WRONG_PRODUCER ÔÇö phase emits an artifact it doesn't own (e.g., APPLY emitting `advisory` which only CLASSIFY produces; or any phase emitting `cancel_token` which is infrastructure-set with empty producer_phases); (4) SCHEMA_VERSION_SKEW ÔÇö reserved for future per-artifact schema bumps (e.g., `generation.2` vs `generation.1`). Each `ArtifactValidation.detail` carries operator-readable diagnosis. **Substrate authority invariants AST-pinned**: imports stdlib + op_context.OperationPhase ONLY (no orchestrator / phase_runner / phase_dispatcher / candidate_generator / iron_gate / change_engine / policy / semantic_guardian / providers / urgency_router); every public function NEVER raises (defensive try/except wraps even custom validators that themselves raise ÔÇö treated as TYPE_MISMATCH); registry-by-key cache invalidatable via `reset_registry_cache_for_tests` for monkey-patch isolation. **32 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_section_35_4_artifact_contract.py`: 2 closed-taxonomy frozen pins (10 ArtifactKind + 6 ValidationOutcome) + 3 registry-coverage pins (covers every PhaseContext slot via AST sweep + key-equals-kind.value redundancy detection + size-pin forces reviewer attention on additions) + 11 validator behavior pins (master-off PASSED_DISABLED + UNKNOWN_KEY rename + UNKNOWN_KEY empty + TYPE_MISMATCH on `generate_retries_remaining`/`t_apply`-string/`t_apply`-bool + WRONG_PRODUCER advisory-from-APPLY + correct CLASSIFY-advisory pass + cancel_token-from-any-phase rejected + custom-validator-raises-treated-as-mismatch) + 5 bundle pins (master-off empty tuple + master-on validates each + non-Mapping TYPE_MISMATCH + first_failure returns first invalid + first_failure returns None on all-OK) + 2 substrate authority pins (no forbidden imports + never-raises spot check) + 3 dispatcher integration AST pins (composes `validate_artifacts_bundle` BEFORE `merge_artifacts` ÔÇö load-bearing positional pin / composes `first_failure` + `PhaseContextError` for strict mode / cites ┬º35 row #4) + 2 functional integration tests (strict mode raises on UNKNOWN_KEY + advisory mode does not raise) + 1 master-off byte-equivalent test (pathological bundle that would FAIL master-on validation produces empty tuple master-off ÔÇö safe-revert contract) + 3 spec round-trip + introspection + 1 substrate provenance pin. **1147/1147 cumulative regression green** across ┬º35 #4 artifact contract + ┬º37 Tier 1 #1+#2+#3 + Phase 8 + P9.5 + Vector #5 + Wave 3 + adversarial cage + scheduler + posture (52 + 15) + sensor_governor (303) + graduation_ledger + 7 v2.82 consumer files. **┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #4 + ┬º3.6.3 priority #4 BOTH flipped Ô£à Shipped 2026-05-09**. Master flag stays default-FALSE per Phase 9 cadence operator binding (graduation flip via 3-clean-soak ladder). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim ("solve the root problem directly ÔÇö without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). Composes existing `phase_dispatcher.PhaseContext` slot declarations (zero parallel state schema), existing `PhaseResult.artifacts` channel (zero parallel transport), existing `merge_artifacts` choke point (single wire-up site). Substrate is the rails; per-artifact `schema_version` enables future shape evolution without breaking the contract ÔÇö bumping `generation.1` ÔåÆ `generation.2` is a single registry edit, not a pipeline refactor. **┬º35 ROW #4 IS THE LAST STRUCTURAL ENGINEERING ITEM**. Autonomy arc remaining work transitions to operator-paced cadence (Phase 9 graduation soaks ~6-9 weeks) + trigger-gated arrival (J-Prime / Reactor-Core repos for ┬º39 Tier 6). The architecture roadmap is structurally complete; remaining is wall-clock validation + external dependencies. See v2.84 below for ┬º37 Tier 1 #2 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.84 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **┬º37 Tier 1 #2 SHIPPED ÔÇö PostureObserver task-death detection consumer wiring**: Closes ┬º37 Tier 1 row #2 ("PostureObserver task-death detection"). **Audit findings**: substrate `posture_health.py` (~571 LOC) was already shipped pre-audit with 4-state closed taxonomy (HEALTHY / DEGRADED_HUNG / DEGRADED_FAILING / TASK_DEAD), pure-function `evaluate_observer_health` classifier, `safe_load_posture` / `safe_load_posture_value` consumer wrappers, debounced `_maybe_publish_degraded_event` SSE publisher, AST-pinned authority invariants, 52 regression tests passing. `invariant_drift_observer.py:438-468` consumer was already wired. `PostureObserver.task_health_snapshot()` heartbeat field exposure was already in place at line 553-589. **4 genuine gaps closed in this slice**: (1) **SensorGovernor consumer wiring** (THE silent-degradation cascade the ┬º37 row names) ÔÇö `_default_posture_fn:318-331` + `_default_signal_bundle_fn:334-351` were calling `get_default_store().load_current()` directly, silently returning stale `_store` state when the observer task is dead-but-still-listed. v2.84 wires both functions to compose canonical `posture_health.safe_load_posture_value` / `safe_load_posture` so a dead PostureObserver degrades the governor to unweighted (1.0├ù) caps ÔÇö equivalent to MAINTAIN safe-default ÔÇö instead of applying weights against frozen state. Substrate-unavailable rollback path preserved (catches `ImportError`, falls through to legacy direct-store read so a missing `posture_health` module never breaks the governor). (2) **Canonical SSE event registration** ÔÇö local constant `EVENT_TYPE_POSTURE_OBSERVER_DEGRADED = "posture_observer_degraded"` existed in `posture_health.py:502-504` but was NOT in `ide_observability_stream._VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset ÔåÆ broker silently rejected publishes from `_maybe_publish_degraded_event`. v2.84 registers the canonical constant; local definition retained for substrate independence (per leaf authority discipline ÔÇö `posture_health` is below `ide_observability_stream` in the import graph and must not couple at module load); AST pin asserts the two definitions agree on string value (drift here would silently drop SSE events). (3) **`/posture health` REPL subcommand** ÔÇö added to `posture_repl.py::dispatch_posture_command` + new `_health()` helper that composes the canonical classifier + `_HELP` block updated. When detection master flag is dormant, returns a notice ("detection dormant ÔÇö set JARVIS_POSTURE_HEALTH_DETECTION_ENABLED=true") rather than fabricating HEALTHY response (operator binding: no fake-healthy). When observer is None, returns `status=TASK_DEAD` (load-bearing distinction ÔÇö operator sees DEAD rather than 5xx). (4) **`GET /observability/posture/health` IDE route** ÔÇö new handler `_handle_posture_health` in `ide_observability.py` registered alongside existing `/observability/posture` + `/observability/posture/history` routes. Composes `evaluate_observer_health(observer.task_health_snapshot())` and returns the verdict via `verdict.to_dict()`. **Version (prior)**: 2.83 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **┬º37 Tier 1 #1 SHIPPED ÔÇö Confidence-drop SSE payload enriched with transition context**: Closes ┬º37 Tier 1 row #1 ("Confidence drop SSE producer wiring"). **Audit findings**: substrate was structurally complete pre-audit ÔÇö `confidence_sse_producer.py` (~741 LOC) ships `ConfidenceTransitionTracker` with state-transition signature dedup mirroring Move 4's drift-signature ring + per-op rate limit + sustained-low milestone tracking; `verification/confidence_observability.py` ships `publish_confidence_drop_event` / `publish_confidence_approaching_event` / `publish_sustained_low_confidence_event`; `doubleword_provider.py:1365-1390` already wires `observe_streaming_verdict` after `_confidence_monitor.evaluate()` (existing AST pin `test_doubleword_provider_imports_observer` locks the wire-up site); event-type vocabulary at `ide_observability_stream.py:471-473` registered. **Genuine gap closed in this slice**: producer holds `prior_verdict` + `consecutive_below` in `TransitionResult` but the publishers didn't accept those fields, so the SSE payload omitted load-bearing transition context. Operators saw "BELOW_FLOOR fired" but couldn't distinguish **fresh OKÔåÆBELOW collapse** (sudden ÔÇö model degraded discontinuously; warrants immediate route escalation) from **APPROACHINGÔåÆBELOW progression** (predicted ÔÇö early-warning fired previously; warrants posture nudge not panic). **Closure** (additive, no parallel logic): (1) `_build_confidence_payload` accepts new optional `prior_verdict` + `consecutive_below` kwargs ÔÇö additive schema, legacy callers omit them and get default values that don't break downstream consumers; permissive enum-or-string handling for `prior_verdict` mirrors existing `verdict` field semantics. (2) `publish_confidence_drop_event` + `publish_confidence_approaching_event` accept the same kwargs and thread them through `_build_confidence_payload`. (3) `ConfidenceTransitionTracker.observe_verdict` at the FIRED_DROP + FIRED_APPROACHING publish blocks (outside the lock so a slow broker doesn't block other observers) passes `prior_verdict=prior.value` + `consecutive_below=consecutive_below_snapshot` to the publishers. Backward-compat preserved end-to-end: `TransitionResult` schema unchanged; existing callers untouched; new fields are additive. **Pre-existing test fix bundled**: `test_observability_pure_stdlib_plus_broker_only` was failing at HEAD (broker-only allowlist hadn't been updated when Move 3 Slice 3's `auto_action_router.record_confidence_verdict` bridge was added); test now allows the canonical `auto_action_router` substrate as a sibling consumer-side bridge ÔÇö same closure shape as v2.82's `test_ledger_only_stdlib_and_adaptation` sync. **13 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_section_37_tier1_1_confidence_drop_payload.py`: 5 payload-schema tests (`prior_verdict` field present + `consecutive_below` field present + legacy-callers-get-defaults + enum-handled + None-handled) + 2 publisher-signature pins (drop + approaching publisher both accept new kwargs) + 2 AST bytes-pins (producer threads `prior_verdict=prior.value` + `consecutive_below=consecutive_below_snapshot` to BOTH `_safe_publish_drop` AND `_safe_publish_approaching` ÔÇö anchored on the publish call sites, not the stats-counting blocks) + 2 functional integration tests (fresh OKÔåÆBELOW: publisher receives `prior_verdict='ok'`; APPROACHINGÔåÆBELOW progression: publisher receives `prior_verdict='approaching_floor'` + injects capturing publishers via `monkeypatch.setattr` per the substrate's `_PublisherSet` test-injection cage) + 2 provenance pins (ÔëÑ3 ┬º37 Tier 1 #1 citations in `confidence_observability` source + ÔëÑ1 in `confidence_sse_producer` source). **961/961 cumulative regression green** across ┬º37 Tier 1 #1 + #3 + Phase 8 + P9.5 + Vector #5 + Wave 3 + adversarial cage + scheduler + posture + graduation_ledger + 7 v2.82 consumer files. **┬º37 Tier 1 row #1 flipped Ô£à Shipped 2026-05-09**. Master flag `JARVIS_CONFIDENCE_SSE_PRODUCER_ENABLED` stays default-FALSE per Phase 9 cadence operator binding (graduation flip via 3-clean-soak ladder, not in this slice). **Version (prior)**: 2.78 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **Vector #9 broader closure ÔÇö mask-discipline consumer-chain pin**: Closes ┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #6 / ┬º3.6.3 priority #5 ("Mask-discipline regression sweep"). Wave 3 v2.25 already closed the SUBSTRATE (`FlagChangeEvent.to_dict()` masks credential-shaped flag names via sha256[:8]+length-token + `_SENSITIVE_NAME_TOKENS` 10-pattern bytes-pinned set). The remaining gap: the dataclass fields (`prev_value` / `next_value`) hold raw values; only the `to_dict()` projection masks. A future consumer doing `e.prev_value` on a credential-shaped flag would bypass the Wave 3 mask. **Audit findings** (2026-05-09): substrate is structurally clean RIGHT NOW ÔÇö 8 access sites internal to `flag_change_emitter.py` (substrate-owned: comparison helpers, property methods, to_dict masking branches) + 2 canonical external consumers (`ide_routes.py` calls `to_dict()` then double-masks via aggressive presence-only `<set>`/`<empty>` markers; `sse_bridge.publish_flag_change_event` uses `getattr(event, "prev_value", None)` and pipes through its own `_mask_flag_value` helper before publish). Zero current violations. **Closure**: structural AST regression sweep `test_mask_discipline_consumer_chain_pinned` walks `backend/` tree (excluding venv/__pycache__), detects every `.prev_value` / `.next_value` Attribute access + every `getattr(_, "prev_value"\|"next_value")` Call, and asserts the receiver file is on a bytes-pinned 2-entry allowlist (`_FLAG_VALUE_ACCESS_ALLOWLIST = frozenset({...flag_change_emitter.py, ...sse_bridge.py})`). Drift requires reviewer attention + explicit allowlist update ÔÇö any new consumer must EITHER compose `to_dict()` (Wave 3 mask) OR be added to the allowlist with explicit cross-review. **5 new regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_wave3_hygiene_2026_05_05.py` (Item 7 section): (1) `test_mask_discipline_consumer_chain_pinned` (the load-bearing AST scan); (2) `test_allowlist_files_actually_use_the_fields` (anti-stale ÔÇö proves no allowlist entry has lost its rationale); (3) `test_allowlist_size_pinned` (forces reviewer attention on size change ÔÇö adding a consumer requires updating BOTH the set AND the size assertion); (4) `test_substrate_uses_mask_helper` (defense-in-depth bytes-pin on `_mask_value` + `_is_sensitive_flag` + `value_masked` field in substrate source); (5) `test_sse_bridge_pipes_through_mask_helper` (defense-in-depth bytes-pin on canonical consumer's getattr+`_mask_flag_value` chain). **IDE route stale comment updated post-Wave-3** (`ide_routes.py:594` previously said "raw FlagChangeEvent.to_dict() echoes verbatim" ÔÇö now correctly cites Wave 3's substrate mask + the route's stricter presence-only double-mask as defense-in-depth). **Test results**: 23/23 mask-discipline (10 sensitive-flag patterns + 5 non-sensitive passthrough + None handling + token set bytes-pin + 5 new structural pins + 1 substrate authority + 1 sse_bridge consumer pin) + **1081/1081 cumulative** across ┬º38.11 (A-F) + ┬º39 Tier-1+2+3+4+5+7 + Wave 3 hygiene + scheduler + canonical sources. **┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #6 + ┬º3.6.3 row #5 both flipped Ô£à Shipped 2026-05-09**. **Version (prior)**: 2.77 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **Vector #11 CLOSED ÔÇö CuriosityScheduler wall-clock ÔåÆ monotonic**: Closes ┬º3.6.2 vector #11 / ┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #8 / ┬º3.6.3 priority #7 (Wave 3 hygiene completion). Bug: `CuriosityScheduler` rate-cap window pruned via `now - 3600.0` and cooldown computed via `now - last_fire_ts` where `now`/`last_fire_ts` were wall-clock seconds (`time.time()`). NTP correction mid-session produced spurious throttle: backward jump of ╬ö seconds ÔåÆ `elapsed = -╬ö` ÔåÆ cooldown sat at `cooldown_s + ╬ö` for hours; forward jump ÔåÆ rolling-hour window evicted true-recent fires aggressively. **Fix** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/adaptation/curiosity_scheduler.py`): dual-track internal state ÔÇö `_fire_history_mono: List[float]` + `_last_fire_mono: Optional[float]` are populated with `time.monotonic()` and drive ALL elapsed-time gating (`_check_rate_cap` + `_check_cooldown` + `_prune_fire_history`); `_last_fire_ts` (wall-clock) preserved as audit-only field driving `SchedulerResult.ts_epoch`. `tick()` accepts new `now_mono` kwarg alongside existing `now_unix` ÔÇö production callers leave both unset (real `time.monotonic()` + `time.time()`); tests inject either or both for determinism. Backward-compat: when only `now_unix` is supplied (existing test API), it is mirrored as `mono_ts` so the 46 pre-existing scheduler tests pass byte-identical. **Bonus fix**: `seconds_since_last_fire` now reports honest monotonic delta and returns `None` (not `ts - 0`) before the first fire ÔÇö closes a legacy bug where the field carried `time.time() Ôëê 1.7e9` as "elapsed" before any fire happened. **10 new regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_curiosity_scheduler.py` (Section J): `TestVector11MonotonicGating` (6 NTP-skew scenarios ÔÇö backward correction does NOT extend cooldown / forward jump does NOT evict history early / `seconds_since_last_fire` uses monotonic delta / pre-fire reports None / production path uses real monotonic / internal state uses `_fire_history_mono` + `_last_fire_mono` fields) + `TestVector11SourcePins` (4 bytes-pinned source assertions: `_prune_fire_history(self, mono_now: float)` signature / `_check_rate_cap(self, mono_now: float)` signature / `tick()` accepts `now_mono: Optional[float] = None` / production fallback uses `mono_ts = time.monotonic()` / "Vector #11" + "NTP-immune" provenance citations in source). **56/56 scheduler tests** (46 backward-compat + 10 new) + **1033/1033 cumulative** across ┬º38.11 (A-F) + ┬º39 Tier-1+2+3+4+5+7 + scheduler + canonical sources. **┬º35 row ­ƒƒí #8 + ┬º3.6.3 row #7 both flipped to Ô£à Shipped 2026-05-09**. Vector #11 is the **third** of the four "small race fixes" closed (Wave 3 already closed vectors #11-elsewhere + #9 + #10 elsewhere; this closes the CuriosityScheduler-specific instance per the Forward Priority Roadmap audit). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim ("solve the root problem directly ÔÇö without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). The fix re-uses canonical `time.monotonic` (Python stdlib, NTP-immune) ÔÇö ZERO new dependencies, ZERO parallel state, ZERO duplicated time math. **NEXT** (autonomy arc): Vector #9 + #10 (~2d remaining hygiene), M10 ArchitectureProposer (~7-10d substrate move closing weak-form ontogeny gap), or Vector #5 cross-session coherence harness (~1-2 wks empirical validation arc). See v2.76 below for ┬º39 Tier-7 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.74 (2026-05-09 ÔÇö **┬º39 Tier-4 SHIPPED ÔÇö Operator's-eye session story (#10) + Memory crystallization timeline (#18)**: Closes ┬º39.5 Tier-4 introspective commitment with two read-only "looking back" surfaces composing canonical sources only. (#9 self-narration already shipped via ┬º38.11-D composition; actual ~7h vs ~13h estimate.) **Surface #10 ÔÇö Session story** new substrate `governance/session_story.py` (~700 LOC pure-stdlib): closed 4-value `StoryArc` enum (DOMINANT_ACTIVITY ­ƒôû / KEY_FINDING Ô£¿ / SETBACK ÔÜá / GROWTH ­ƒî▒) bytes-pinned to glyph map; frozen ┬º33.5 `StoryBeat` (arc + sentence + weight) + `SessionStory` (session_id + duration_human + cost_human + stop_reason + beats + aggregated_at_unix) with `beat_for_arc(arc)` accessor + symmetric `to_dict`. **Aggregator** `aggregate_session_story(n_sessions=)` composes canonical `last_session_summary.get_default_summary().load(n_sessions)` ÔÇö ZERO parallel summary parsing ÔÇö and runs each `SessionRecord` through `_build_beats(rec)` pure-function which derives DOMINANT_ACTIVITY from attempted/completed stats with success-pct narrative ("You ran 23 ops over 35m ÔÇö 20 completed (87% success)"); KEY_FINDING from canonical `last_apply_*` + `last_verify_*` + `last_commit_hash` fields ("Applied 3 files in op abc ÔÇö (12/12 tests passed) ÔÇö committed as 5ce6327e."); SETBACK from failed + cancelled counts; GROWTH from `convergence_state` + `drift_status` + `drift_ratio`. `_format_duration_human(seconds)` produces "0s"/"45s"/"2m"/"1h 2m"; `_format_cost_human(cost)` produces "free"/"$0.0042"/"$0.42". Renderer `format_session_story(story)` produces journal-style multi-line; `format_session_stories(stories)` joins multi-session collection. **4 AST pins**: master_default_false / authority_asymmetry / arc_taxonomy_4_values / **composes_canonical_last_session_summary**. 2 FlagRegistry seeds (master + max_sessions clamped 1..10). **Surface #18 ÔÇö Memory crystallization timeline** new substrate `governance/memory_crystallization.py` (~830 LOC pure-stdlib): closed 4-value `CrystalAge` enum (NASCENT ┬À / FORMING ÔûÆ / SOLID Ôûô / CRYSTALLIZED Ôûê) bytes-pinned thresholds; first-match-wins `_age_for_insight(evidence_count, confidence)` bucketing (evÔëÑ10 + confÔëÑ0.8 ÔåÆ CRYSTALLIZED / evÔëÑ5 + confÔëÑ0.6 ÔåÆ SOLID / evÔëÑ2 ÔåÆ FORMING / else NASCENT); bytes-pinned `_CANONICAL_CATEGORIES = ("failure_pattern", "success_pattern", "file_fragility", "timing_pattern")` matching canonical MemoryInsight schema (consciousness/types.py:MemoryInsight). Frozen ┬º33.5 `Crystal` (insight_id + category + age + content + confidence + evidence_count + last_seen_iso + last_seen_unix) + `CrystalLayer` (category + crystals + by_age) + `CrystalTimeline` (aggregated_at_unix + total_insights + earliest/latest_unix + layers + by_age) with `cell_for_phase`/`layer_for_category` accessors + symmetric `to_dict`. **Reader** `_read_insights()` parses canonical on-disk `.jarvis/ouroboros/consciousness/insights.jsonl` directly (matches harness.py `_consciousness_dir = repo_path / .jarvis / ouroboros / consciousness`) ÔÇö ZERO MemoryEngine import (which is async). NEVER raises: bad JSON lines skipped / missing file ÔåÆ empty / missing fields ÔåÆ defensive defaults / malformed timestamps ÔåÆ 0.0. Aggregator `aggregate_crystal_timeline()` buckets by canonical category (defensive "other" bucket for future schema drift), sorts each layer by `last_seen_unix` desc, computes per-layer + global `by_age` distributions, fills `earliest_unix`/`latest_unix` window. Renderer `format_crystal_timeline(crystals_per_layer=5)` produces geological-strata view: top-line `­ƒ¬¿ Memory crystallization timeline:` + age summary `nascent=N ┬À forming=M ┬À solid=K ┬À crystallized=L` + per-category strata with category-glyph (ÔÜá/Ô£ô/­ƒ¬¿/ÔÅ▒/ÔÇó) + age-tinted glyphs (┬À/ÔûÆ/Ôûô/Ôûê) + content snippet. **5 AST pins**: master_default_false / authority_asymmetry (forbids orchestrator + risk_tier_floor + candidate_generator + **MemoryEngine** ÔÇö substrate must read on-disk directly) / age_taxonomy_4_values / **canonical_categories_pinned** (bytes-pin: 4 canonical MemoryInsight categories must appear in `_CANONICAL_CATEGORIES` source ÔÇö drift requires explicit pin update) / **composes_canonical_insights_path** (bytes-pin: must reference `.jarvis` + `insights.jsonl` paths). 2 FlagRegistry seeds (master + max_insights clamped 10..5000). **Combined `/story` REPL** (per Tier-3 precedent) auto-discovered via ┬º33.3 naming-cage: `/story {session [N]|crystals [N]|status|help}` (4 subcommands; help bypasses both master gates per discoverability invariant). **Two new canonical SSE events** registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset: `EVENT_TYPE_SESSION_STORY_RENDERED = "session_story_rendered"` + `EVENT_TYPE_MEMORY_CRYSTALLIZATION_AGGREGATED = "memory_crystallization_aggregated"` (bounded payload ÔÇö layer-summary only, NOT raw crystal bodies). **Sub-flag granularity**: story master `JARVIS_SESSION_STORY_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ┬º33.1 + 1 tunable; crystallization master `JARVIS_MEMORY_CRYSTALLIZATION_ENABLED` default-FALSE + 1 tunable. **75 regression tests** covering both surfaces: story master + 5 truthy + 4-value StoryArc + lenient-coerce + duration/cost helpers + arc_weight clamped + SessionStory to_dict + aggregate master-off / master-on-real-LSS + format master-off / explicit-story-renders-glyphs + 4 AST pin canonical-source pass + 4 synthetic regressions + flag count + crystal master + 5 truthy + 4-value CrystalAge + 9 _age_for_insight parametrized + invalid + canonical_categories_pinned regression + Crystal/CrystalLayer to_dict + layer_for_category lookup + aggregate master-off / **synthetic-jsonl-end-to-end** (3-row plant verifies CRYSTALLIZED+SOLID+FORMING bucketing) / **malformed-jsonl-handles-gracefully** (verifies bad lines skipped without raising) / missing-file-empty + format master-off / synthetic-renders-glyphs + 5 AST pin canonical-source pass + 5 synthetic regressions + flag count + 8 `/story` REPL tests + 3 canonical-source smokes (`EVENT_TYPE_SESSION_STORY_RENDERED` registered / `EVENT_TYPE_MEMORY_CRYSTALLIZATION_AGGREGATED` registered / `get_default_summary` callable). **End-to-end smoke** against real canonical sources: session story aggregated real session `bt-2026-05-09-060458` from LSS into 4-beat narrative (35m duration, $0.23 cost, idle_timeout stop, INSUFFICIENT_DATA convergence + ok drift); **Version (prior)**: 2.73 (2026-05-08 ÔÇö **┬º39 Tier-3 SHIPPED ÔÇö Op trajectory predictor (#4) + Risk-aware command preview (#19)**: Closes ┬º39.5 Tier-3 intelligent commitment with two predictive surfaces composing canonical sources only. (#11 capability gap proposals already shipped via ┬º38.11-E composition.) **Surface #4 ÔÇö Op trajectory predictor** new substrate `governance/op_trajectory_predictor.py` (~770 LOC pure-stdlib): closed 4-value `TrajectoryConfidence` enum (HIGH ­ƒÄ» / MEDIUM ­ƒÄ▓ / LOW ÔØô / UNKNOWN Ôï») bytes-pinned to `_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLDS` table (0.70/0.40 ÔÇö AST regression locks numeric values); first-match-wins `_score_to_confidence(score, sufficient_samples=)` bucketing with sample-size override (insufficient ÔåÆ UNKNOWN); frozen ┬º33.5 `TrajectoryPrediction` artifact (op_id + confidence + confidence_score + similar_op_count + similar_op_kind + median_duration_s + p90_duration_s + estimated_completion_at_unix + elapsed_so_far_s + diagnostic) with symmetric `to_dict`. **Predictor** `predict_trajectory(op_id, op_kind=None)` composes canonical `op_block_buffer.blocks_by_state(COMMITTED)` for history sample + canonical `find_by_op_id(op_id)` for active-op timing ÔÇö ZERO parallel duration ledger. Confidence score = geometric-mean(sample_size_score ├ù variance_tightness) where variance_tightness = `1 - clamp(stdev/mean)`; pure NumPy-free statistics via stdlib `statistics`. Optional `op_kind` filter matches `subagent_kind` field with fallback to all-ops baseline if zero matches. ETA computed as `now + max(0, median_duration - elapsed_so_far)`. Pure p90 percentile via linear interpolation. Renderer `format_trajectory_prediction(p)` produces one-liner: `­ƒÄ» Op 019d: 73% confidence, ~2m ETA based on 12 similar ops`. **5 AST pins**: master_default_false / authority_asymmetry / confidence_taxonomy_4_values / **composes_canonical_op_block_buffer** / **confidence_thresholds_canonical** (bytes-pin: 0.70/0.40 thresholds + `_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLDS` table ÔÇö drift requires explicit pin update). 3 FlagRegistry seeds (master + min_samples [clamped 1..50] + history_limit [clamped 5..500]). **Surface #19 ÔÇö Risk-aware command preview** new substrate `governance/risk_command_preview.py` (~660 LOC pure-stdlib): closed 4-value `PreviewVerdict` enum (SAFE Ô£ô / NOTIFY ÔÜá / APPROVAL ­ƒöÆ / BLOCKED Ô£ù) mapping risk-tier-floor names via bytes-pinned `_FLOOR_TO_VERDICT` (canonical `safe_auto`/`notify_apply`/`approval_required`/`blocked` floor names). Frozen ┬º33.5 `CommandPreview` artifact (command_summary + predicted_route + route_reason + predicted_floor + verdict + governor_emergency + estimated_cost_usd + estimated_duration_s + target_file_count + diagnostic). Frozen `_PreviewContext` synthetic dataclass duck-typed for the canonical `UrgencyRouter.classify(ctx)` ÔÇö exposes only the 7 read-fields the classifier accesses (signal_urgency / signal_source / task_complexity / target_files / cross_repo / provider_route / provider_route_reason); ZERO clone of OperationContext. **Previewer** `preview_command(*, command_summary, signal_urgency, signal_source, task_complexity, target_files, cross_repo)` composes canonical (1) `UrgencyRouter().classify(synthetic_ctx)` for predicted route + reason ÔÇö same pure deterministic classifier every real op flows through; (2) `risk_tier_floor.recommended_floor()` for current cage stance; (3) `sensor_governor.is_emergency_brake()` for verdict bump (governor_emergency=True ÔåÆ BLOCKED); (4) bytes-pinned `_ROUTE_COST_USD` + `_ROUTE_DURATION_S` per-route estimate tables (drawn from CLAUDE.md ┬º"Urgency-Aware Provider Routing" canonical numbers ÔÇö IMMEDIATE $0.03/30s / STANDARD $0.005/60s / COMPLEX $0.015/120s / BACKGROUND $0.002/90s / SPECULATIVE $0.001/5s). Renderer `format_command_preview(p)` multi-line: verdict glyph + traffic light + route + cost + duration + file count + emergency warning. **5 AST pins**: master_default_false / authority_asymmetry (forbids orchestrator + candidate_generator imports ÔÇö risk_tier_floor IS allowed as canonical read-only source) / verdict_taxonomy_4_values / **composes_canonical_urgency_router** / **composes_canonical_risk_tier_floor**. 1 FlagRegistry seed. **Combined `/forecast` REPL** (per ┬º38.11 precedent of related surfaces under one verb) auto-discovered via ┬º33.3 naming-cage: `/forecast {trajectory <op-id> [kind]|command <urgency> <source> <complexity> [files...] [--cross-repo]|status|help}` (4 subcommands; help bypasses both master gates per discoverability invariant). **Two new canonical SSE events** registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset: `EVENT_TYPE_TRAJECTORY_PREDICTED = "trajectory_predicted"` + `EVENT_TYPE_COMMAND_PREVIEW_RENDERED = "command_preview_rendered"`. **Sub-flag granularity**: trajectory master `JARVIS_OP_TRAJECTORY_PREDICTOR_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ┬º33.1 + 2 tunables (min_samples / history_limit); preview master `JARVIS_RISK_COMMAND_PREVIEW_ENABLED` default-FALSE (single master ÔÇö no sub-flags needed; the preview is single-purpose). **87 regression tests**: trajectory master flag default-FALSE + 5 truthy + clamped tunables + 4-value taxonomy / 7 score-to-confidence parametrized + invalid-stringÔåÆUNKNOWN + nan-falls-through-to-LOW (documents NaN comparison semantics) + percentile pure / TrajectoryPrediction to_dict + predict master-off / no-history-UNKNOWN / with-history-correct-sample-count + format master-off / unknown-confidence / high-confidence (verifies 85% + ­ƒÄ» + sample count) + 5 AST pin canonical-source pass + 5 synthetic regressions + flag count + preview master + 5 truthy + 4-value PreviewVerdict + 6 floorÔåÆverdict parametrized + governor-emergency-overrides-to-BLOCKED + CommandPreview to_dict + preview master-off / high-test-failure-routes-IMMEDIATE-with-$0.03-cost / voice_human-IMMEDIATE / ai_miner-low-urgency-routes-BACKGROUND / heavy_code-routes-COMPLEX / cross_repo-routes-IMMEDIATE-with-cross_repo-diagnostic + format master-off / renders + 5 AST pin canonical-source pass + 5 synthetic regressions + flag count + 11 `/forecast` REPL subcommand tests (incl. trajectory-master-off-blocks / no-op-id-fails / master-on-renders + command-master-off-blocks / too-few-args-fails / master-on-routes-IMMEDIATE / files+cross-repo) + 3 canonical-source smokes (`EVENT_TYPE_TRAJECTORY_PREDICTED` registered / `EVENT_TYPE_COMMAND_PREVIEW_RENDERED` registered / `ProviderRoute` still 5 values lockstep). **End-to-end smoke** against real canonical sources: trajectory predictor reads OpBlockBuffer with 5 planted COMMITTED ops + 1 active op ÔåÆ returns 57% MEDIUM confidence with 5 similar ops sample, renders as `­ƒÄ▓ Op op-active-1: 57% confidence, ~0s ETA based on 5 similar ops`; fields: kind / signal_source / summary / rationale / priority_hint (clamped 0..1) / emitted_at_unix / decision / decided_at_unix / decision_note / schema_version. **Symmetric to_dict / from_dict** projection (returns None on defensive parse failure; NEVER raises). **`ProactiveProposalLedger`** thread-safe singleton ÔÇö bounded ring (env: `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_RING_SIZE`, default 64, clamped 8..512) with drop-oldest eviction; idempotent on duplicate `proposal_id` (returns existing record); accept/reject state machine with terminal-idempotency guarantee (second `accept_proposal(pid)` on already-ACCEPTED is no-op `True`; `reject_proposal(pid)` on ACCEPTED returns `False`); `expire_stale(expiry_s=)` sweep marks PENDING proposals older than `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_EXPIRY_SECONDS` (default 24h, min 60s) as EXPIRED. **Producer-bridge ┬º33.2** `emit_proposal(kind=, signal_source=, summary=, rationale=, priority_hint=)` returns `proposal_id` on success or `None` on master/sub-flag-off ÔÇö lazy-importable from each of the 4 canonical producers; NEVER raises. **Operator-decision API** `accept_proposal(pid, note=)` + `reject_proposal(pid, note=)` proxies through canonical ledger. **┬º33.4 flock'd JSONL persistence layer (sub-flag opt-in)** ÔÇö when `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_PERSISTENCE_ENABLED=true`, every proposal lifecycle event (record / accept / reject / expire) appends a line to `.jarvis/proactive_proposals.jsonl` via canonical `cross_process_jsonl.flock_append_line` (composes the ┬º33.4 reusable substrate); help bypasses master gate; `expire` triggers manual sweep; `status` reports per-kind + per-decision counts via canonical aggregator). **5 AST pins**: master_default_false (synthetic regression on premature flip fires) / authority_asymmetry (forbidden imports of orchestrator/risk_tier_floor/candidate_generator) / **proposal_kind_taxonomy_4_values** (4 canonical producer slots ÔÇö adding a kind requires a new producer) / **proposal_decision_taxonomy_4_values** (PENDING + 3 terminal) / **composes_canonical_signal_source** (bytes-pin: `ProactiveProposal` MUST carry `signal_source` field ÔÇö required by ┬º38.11.5a row 5 reconciliation envelope shape; synthetic regression on missing fires). **5 FlagRegistry seeds**. **52 regression tests**: master flag default-FALSE + 5 truthy parametrized + sub-flag composition (2 cases) + 4-value ProposalKind / 4-value ProposalDecision / decision is_terminal property / lenient-coerce + ProactiveProposal to_dict / from_dict round-trip / from_dict-invalid-returns-None / priority_hint clamped 0..1 + emit master-off / master-on (verifies sha256[:12] id) / idempotent-on-duplicate + accept/reject decision API + decide-unknown-id / decide-terminal-idempotent + expire_stale-marks-old / ring-eviction (drops oldest at capacity) + pending_proposals filter + format panel (master-off / no-pending / 4-kinds-render-all-glyphs) + `/proposals` REPL (unmatched / help / panel-blocks-master-off / panel-master-on / show-unknown-id / accept-via-command / status / expire / unknown-subcommand) + 5 AST pin canonical-source pass + 5 synthetic regressions + 2 canonical-source smokes (`EVENT_TYPE_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_EMITTED` registered / `intent.signals.SignalSource` importable). **End-to-end smoke**: 4 producers emit one proposal each ÔåÆ 4 panel rows render with all 4 glyphs (­ƒö¡/­ƒº®/­ƒÆí/­ƒÅø);
